Title: Reporting Engineer
Description:
Founded in 1999, the company has a premium listing on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange and is focused on regulated and regulating markets across its B2B business. By leveraging its proprietary technology, Playtech delivers innovative products and services to ensure a safe, engaging and entertaining gaming experience.



As the gaming industry's leading technology company, it combines business intelligence-driven software, services, content, and platform technology to drive excellence and innovation across the sector. Read more about who we are and what we do here: www.playtechpeople.com and www.playtech.com



Here at Playtech, we genuinely believe that people are our biggest asset. Diverse thoughts, experiences, and individual characteristics enrich our work environment and lead to better business decisions. Embracing differences and maintaining transparency in our processes is the core of Playtech's overall commitment to responsible business practices.



Ready to level up your career?



Playtech’s IMS team is on the lookout for a diligent, proactive, and detail‑oriented Reporting Engineer with strong analytical and system thinking skills





Job Description



Your influential mission. You will…



Design and develop high‑performing SQL queries and reporting solutions that handle large and complex datasets
Troubleshoot and optimize existing reports, ensuring faster performance, improved scalability, and stronger data integrity
Collaborate with cross‑functional teams to understand reporting needs and translate them into efficient technical solutions
Guarantee data consistency by analyzing dependencies between reports and proactively identifying potential issues
Continuously improve reporting processes, introducing automation and best practices to enhance efficiency


Qualifications



Components for success. You…



Have extensive knowledge of writing efficient SQL queries, spotting problematic areas, and optimizing them for performance
Possess strong experience working with large datasets and designing queries that balance speed and accuracy
Understand relational databases and database architecture at a deep level.
Demonstrate analytical thinking, attention to detail, effective time management, and strong collaboration skills
Are proactive and systematic in your approach, always seeking ways to improve processes and outcomes

You'll get extra points for…



Hands‑on experience with JavaScript, Python, or Java, especially in integrating reporting solutions with broader systems
Familiarity with data visualization tools or libraries that enhance reporting clarity
Ability to automate repetitive reporting tasks and streamline workflows

Thrive in a culture that values…



Collaboration and teamwork across diverse functions, where knowledge sharing is encouraged
Proactivity and ownership in solving complex problems, with freedom to propose new ideas
Systematic approaches that ensure scalability, reliability, and long‑term sustainability of solutions
Continuous improvement, curiosity, and a passion for learning new technologies
A supportive environment that balances high performance with personal growth


Additional Information



IMS UNIT



At the heart of our product ecosystem lies the Information Management Solution (IMS) - a powerful, fully‑integrated suite of tools designed to empower operators in every aspect of their business. Built by business people for business people, IMS provides intuitive solutions that help clients define, execute, and continuously optimize their strategies.



This platform enables seamless management of the player base, allowing operators to:



Optimize marketing spend and maximize ROI.



Unlock cross‑selling and conversion opportunities.



Strengthen player loyalty and lifetime value.



The IMS is an open, flexible system that integrates with any third‑party gaming products, tools, or services, creating a unified and centralized operation. Its cross‑platform capability ensures players enjoy a single login across all games and platforms, delivering a smooth experience anytime, anywhere.



For our teams, IMS is not just a product - it’s the backbone of innovation. Working within this unit means contributing to a solution that directly impacts business performance, player engagement, and the scalability of global operations.



Playtech is an equal opportunities employer. Our mission is to welcome everyone and create inclusive teams. We celebrate differences and encourage everyone to join us and be themselves at work.

Title: Web Analyst
Description:
We're looking for a Web Analyst with strong GA4, GTM, and SQL expertise who can turn data into actionable insights. You'll build dashboards, analyze performance, and tell compelling data stories that help teams make smarter decisions. If you're independent, collaborative, and enjoy working in an inclusive environment, we'd love to hear from you.




In this role, you are going to:

Establish, maintain, review, and continuously optimize Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager (GTM) configurations to ensure absolute data accuracy and integrity
Analyze and optimize dataLayer coverage and structure to ensure robust, scalable data collection
Create and implement measurement strategies perfectly aligned with business goals, defining custom dimensions, metrics, and advanced event tracking
Utilize GA4 with all its functionalities to perform deep-dive analyses, uncover trends, and build customized reports and dashboards that turn complex data into actionable business insights
Apply familiarity with modern marketing and tracking practices to safely implement, troubleshoot, and thoroughly test tracking scripts
Partner with marketing, product, and development teams to promote data-driven decision-making and ensure tracking specs are met
Collaborate with cross-functional teams on data analysis and segmentation; support A/B testing initiatives where relevant
Contribute to broader business insights analysis, working alongside the analytics team to connect web data to wider business performance questions
Keep up-to-date with the latest privacy regulations, GA4 features, and analytics best practices




To be successful in this role, you need to have:

Demonstrated experience setting up, maintaining, reviewing, and optimizing complex Google Tag Manager and GA4 environments
Strong understanding of dataLayer architecture, including the ability to analyze coverage gaps and guide developers on optimization
Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to leverage the full suite of GA4 functionalities to answer complex business questions
Hands-on experience implementing various marketing and tracking scripts, with a disciplined approach to testing and QA
Strong SQL skills for querying and analyzing raw GA4 export data
Excellent communication skills and proficiency in spoken and written English - capable of translating intricate tracking setups and raw data into clear, stakeholder-friendly insight




Preferred skills and experience:

Familiarity with iGaming metrics and KPIs
Experience working with Google BigQuery
Knowledge of Google Data Studio / Looker Studio for creating automated, dynamic dashboards
Familiarity with Google Search Console (GSC) to connect search performance with on-site user behavior
Sufficient knowledge of A/B testing and CRO methodologies to support other roles with advanced data validation and analysis
Solid understanding of web development technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and how they impact tracking and DOM scraping
Google Analytics or Google Tag Manager certifications




We will show our gratitude by:

Offering working tools of employees´ own choice - Company makes sure everyone feels comfortable in the office and has all the necessary tools one might need
Creating an exciting and fun international English-speaking environment
Working in a modern office in Ülemiste City
Organizing different company and team events
Offering extra sports compensation via Stebby + private health insurance
Creating an opportunity to learn and develop
Offering a competitive salary based on experience and a benefits package




If this seems like a challenge you are looking for, apply and let’s have a chat!




Sounds great but what do we do?

We are a growing company focused on the use of data to benefit e-commerce operations. Our Data Scientists provide decision-making assistance and models to improve operational efficiency and customer experience. The Analytics team provides insights on specific topics. Our Business Intelligence team makes information accessible. Together with the right technology we deliver the foundation for highly automated and effective CRM solutions.

Learn more about NabuMinds: https://nabuminds.com




As we strive to achieve our mission we reaffirm our commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). We are proud to employ people of all backgrounds who possess the talent, energy, and focus to accelerate our vision forward and to tell our story. Each employee is encouraged to bring their whole self to work, knowing that NabuMinds values individuality as much as we value collaboration.

Title: Data Analyst II Riga, Latvia
Description:
About Us

GoCardless is a global bank payment company. Over 100,000 businesses, from start-ups to household names, use GoCardless to collect and send payments through direct debit, real-time payments and open banking.

GoCardless processes US$130bn+ of payments annually, across 30+ countries; helping customers collect and send both recurring and one-off payments, without the chasing, stress or expensive fees. We use AI-powered solutions to improve payment success and reduce fraud. And, with open banking connectivity to over 2,500 banks, we help our customers make faster, more informed decisions.

We are headquartered in the UK with offices in London and Leeds, and additional locations in Australia, France, Ireland, Latvia, Portugal and the United States.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

Payment Intelligence

Our mission is to make GoCardless the best way to pay and be paid. Our ambition is to dramatically improve payment performance and safeguard our customers from bad outcomes like payment fraud and failures. You will help find data opportunities and areas to improve our payment intelligence products. You should be comfortable with large data sets, customer data, and machine learning principles. You’ll work alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Data Scientists in our two core Payment Intelligence teams to provide critical insights and help drive product direction.

Our existing Payment Intelligence Products are already one of the key reasons why customers choose GoCardless. These include Protect+, which defends customers from fraud, and Success+, which helps customers intelligently recover failed payments. You will need to own our understanding of these products and identify insights and opportunities to help us develop new intelligent features.

What You'll Do

At GoCardless analytics is at the core of everything we do and drives business performance through data insight. You will play a critical role partnering closely with stakeholders across the business (including product, operations, marketing, and commercial teams) in order to deliver actionable insights to influence decisions at every level of the organisation. The role is focussed on identifying value and driving change throughout the business derived from insights. Your insights will be turned into actions that will help drive the future of GoCardless. You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.


Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)


What Excites You


Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.


What Excites Us


You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!


Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff


Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)


Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

Payment Intelligence

Our mission is to make GoCardless the best way to pay and be paid. Our ambition is to dramatically improve payment performance and safeguard our customers from bad outcomes like payment fraud and failures. You will help find data opportunities and areas to improve our payment intelligence products. You should be comfortable with large data sets, customer data, and machine learning principles. You’ll work alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Data Scientists in our two core Payment Intelligence teams to provide critical insights and help drive product direction.

Our existing Payment Intelligence Products are already one of the key reasons why customers choose GoCardless. These include Protect+, which defends customers from fraud, and Success+, which helps customers intelligently recover failed payments. You will need to own our understanding of these products and identify insights and opportunities to help us develop new intelligent features.

What You'll Do

At GoCardless analytics is at the core of everything we do and drives business performance through data insight. You will play a critical role partnering closely with stakeholders across the business (including product, operations, marketing, and commercial teams) in order to deliver actionable insights to influence decisions at every level of the organisation. The role is focussed on identifying value and driving change throughout the business derived from insights. Your insights will be turned into actions that will help drive the future of GoCardless. You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.

  • Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
  • Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
  • Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
  • Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
  • Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)

What Excites You

  • Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
  • Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
  • Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
  • Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
  • Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
  • Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.

What Excites Us

  • You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
  • You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
  • You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
  • You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
  • You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
  • You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!

Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff

  • Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
  • Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
  • Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
  • Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
  • Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
  • Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
  • Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)

Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

Title: Product analyst
HTML:
SIMPLE is a successful mobile product with over 15 million unique downloads, more than 300K 5-star reviews, and over 50% year-over-year revenue growth.

It offers judgment-free, gentle guidance toward balanced nutrition, a healthy relationship with food, and ultimately, improved health and well-being. Built with flexibility and convenience in mind, the app is a safe and supportive space to get actionable feedback, learn, and increase confidence. SIMPLE’s method is shaped by a global team of nutrition, behavior change, digital health, and medical experts. The journey is enhanced through Avo, a personal wellness assistant within the app that provides timely suggestions and real-time answers.

With SIMPLE as a partner in their pocket, users feel cared for and empowered to embrace — and stick to — new healthy habits. To learn more, visit simple.life.

Right now we are looking for a talented Product Analyst who enjoys exploring product metrics, uncovering insights, and helping drive growth through analytical thinking.

This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, takes initiative in analyzing trends, and provides actionable recommendations.

Push the pace of innovation and build a future of a healthier world with us!

What We’re Looking For

  • Advanced SQL skills – ability to write complex queries using joins, window functions, CTEs, and aggregations; optimize query performance for large datasets;
  • Experience working with ETL pipelines and building data workflows (Airflow, dbt, or similar); understanding how to ensure data quality, reliability, and timely delivery.
  • Basic Python for data analysis and automation;
  • Experience with any BI tool (Looker, Superset, Tableau, Power BI, or similar);
  • Understanding of fundamental statistics, A/B tests, and experimentation concepts (proper test design, power calculation, post-hoc analysis, etc.)
  • Ability to structure and interpret data, ensuring accuracy and meaningful insights;
  • Strong communication skills to collaborate effectively with product, marketing, and other teams;
  • A proactive mindset—someone who enjoys diving into data, identifying trends, and proposing opportunities for growth.

Nice To Have

  • Experience working as a product or data analyst specifically in a subscription-based service (SaaS, mobile subscriptions, OTT, etc.);
  • Experience running pricing A/B experiments.

What You'll Do

  • Assist in evaluating experiments and interpreting results to optimize product initiatives;
  • Participate in evaluating pricing experiments and interpreting results to inform product and marketing strategies;
  • Work on improving the existing LTV forecasting model and develop additional predictive models as needed;
  • Build reports and dashboards to provide visibility into product trends and opportunities;
  • Identify and explore potential growth levers based on data-driven insights;

Perks And Benefits

  • Competitive salary package commensurate with experience;
  • Remote, in-office, and hybrid work opportunities;
  • The equipment you need to do your job;
  • A premium SIMPLE subscription;
  • 21 days annual leave, plus bank holidays (those observed where you live);
  • Flexible hours. We focus on your results, not how long you spend at your desk.

About Our Values

  • Think deeper: We understand that in order to grow we need to make all our decisions reality-based and change our opinion based on what we learn. We appreciate data coming in various forms – quantitative and qualitative, feedback from users and colleagues, and strong and weak signals.We treat data as the main source for leveraging insights and expect people at every level to have conversations that start with data.
  • Focus on impact: Results and speed matter. When we are competing to become an A-player in the digital health market, we don’t have the luxury of deliberation. We need to make decisions and changes quickly and, swiftly learn from our mistakes.We prioritize what will have the greatest impact and aren’t distracted by anything else. We create products that benefit users while we are meeting our metrics.
  • Take ownership: We seek to improve all facets of our company even in ways beyond our job description. We seek and take responsibility for our actions and their impact. We value and set high expectations for our own work so that it can add to the overall quality and innovation results of the team. Each one of us is empowered to make this company a success, take the lead to resolve disagreements and systemic issues.
  • Push the limits: We encourage our team to explore new ideas, challenge conventional thinking, and continuously improve work. This mindset can lead to breakthroughs in product development, improved operational efficiency, and increased competitiveness in the market. We believe that a culture and mindset of constantly striving to exceed existing standards, boundaries, or expectations that include innovation, experimentation, and a willingness to take risks, can bring us success. We don't accept what someone says as truth if we disagree with it, no matter what authority that person has in the company and express ourselves directly, not through back channels. We challenge ideas, from policy to product decisions, and always seek to understand the reason behind what we do.
  • Be a Championship Team: As a part of the championship team, you must improve your own performance constantly also know your teammates, their talents and skills and be focused on a common goal and how to achieve it together. We hold each other accountable for our contribution to the shared success or failure, and we constantly look for ways to help our colleagues to improve and for us to perform better as a team. We collaborate within the team in order to compete with challengers in the outside world. We build relationships of trust. We provide our teammates with the autonomy and support they need to deliver their part of the goal.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

This is a great time to join Jacobs’ Dublin team as we scale our analytics capability within transportation. You’d be working on BusConnects, a major infrastructure programme that will directly improve daily commutes and make the city more sustainable. It’s a real greenfield opportunity as the team is still maturing, so you’ll help shape processes, influence how analytics is done, and have a tangible impact from day one. There’s strong growth in the pipeline, with leadership heavily investing in this space.

With a chance to make a real difference to real peoples day-to-day lives, why wouldn't you want to make an impact?

Day to Day


Frame ambiguous business problems into structured data analysis questions and testable hypotheses
Apply core Analytics techniques including descriptive, inferential, and predictive analysis
Perform statistical analysis using regression, hypothesis testing, sampling, and confidence intervals
Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in complex datasets
Build reproducible analytical workflows using Python and notebook environments
Use AI tools to enhance data analysis, code generation, and insight discovery with careful validation
Query, validate, and manipulate data using SQL across multiple data sources
Create clear data visualisations and insight-led narratives tailored to different audiences
Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty
Collaborate with stakeholders to refine data requirements and ensure outputs are relevant and accurate
Assess data quality, bias, and limitations, and clearly articulate what the data can and cannot support
Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks at all times



Technical Stack


Python for data analysis including pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels
SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation
Jupyter or similar notebook tools for reproducible data analysis
Azure-based data platforms including Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
Data pipeline tooling such as Azure Data Factory
AI and copilot tools to support and accelerate analytical workflows
Data visualisation using matplotlib, seaborn, Plotly or similar
Version control and CI/CD practices using Git



What’s In It For You


Apply your Data Analyst skills to deliver insight on major infrastructure and transformation programmes
Accelerate your career as a Data Analyst in a high-growth, high-visibility environment
Work alongside experienced analysts, data professionals, and domain experts
Develop both your technical data skills and your stakeholder-facing consulting capability
Be part of a collaborative culture where data-driven thinking is valued and encouraged
Gain exposure to diverse datasets, sectors, and analytical challenges
Learn to apply data analysis in complex, real-world environments with measurable impact
Take ownership of your work and grow your capability in a supportive, forward-thinking team



Benefits


Competitive salary aligned to experience
33 days paid holiday per annum with the option to purchase more
Stock purchase options
Inclusive employee networks and diverse community
Family planning support including fertility services
Enhanced parental leave and support programmes
Income protection for long-term security
40 hour working week with flexible working patterns
Wellbeing hub with access to apps such as Calm and Daily Burn
Professional development, paid memberships, and CPD opportunities
Charity match funding, volunteering time, and donations



#Digital&Data

Here's what you'll need

Key Skills & Experience Required


Strong experience with
SQL
Python
Microsoft Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
Exposure to AI and advanced analytics
Familiarity with Generative AI (AI augmentation concepts)
Experience using analytical tools that support AI-driven insights
Communication & Storytelling
Strong ability to communicate insights clearly
Capable of translating data into meaningful business stories
Data Management & Governance
Solid understanding of data quality and assurance principles
Knowledge of data completeness and data lineage
Engineering Best Practices
Experience with version control (e.g. Git)
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment practices
Cloud Environment
Primary focus on Microsoft Azure
Experience with AWS or Google BigQuery is transferable
Leadership & Growth
Will be expected to support and mentor others

Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees.

With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs.

We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here.

Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles.

As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

Title: BI datu analītiķis/-e
HTML:
Biznesa datu analītiķis/-e

Baložu iela 20a, Āgenskalns· Hibrīds

  • Pilna laika

Dati ir daudz vērtīgāki par skaitļiem – tie palīdz pieņemt gudrākus lēmumus. Ja Tevi aizrauj un uzrunā Power BI, SQL un datu analīze, kā arī vēlies būt daļa no uzņēmuma datu attīstības un veidot risinājumus, kas ietekmē biznesa rezultātus, pievienojies MADARA Cosmetics finanšu departamentam.

MĒS TEV UZTICĒSIM

  • Izstrādāt, uzturēt un pilnveidot Power BI atskaites, informācijas paneļus (dashboards) un datu modeļus;
  • rakstīt un optimizēt SQL vaicājumus lielu datu apjomu apstrādei no datubāzēm un datu noliktavas;
  • līdzdarboties uzņēmuma pārejā uz mākoņa datu noliktavu (Google BigQuery), sadarbojoties ar datu inženieriem datu avotu un Power BI atskaišu migrācijā, kā arī testēt un validēt rezultātus;
  • sadarboties ar kolēģiem un vadību, lai noskaidrotu vajadzības, definētu rādītājus un sagatavotu skaidras, praktiski izmantojamas atskaites;
  • rūpēties par datu kvalitāti, konsekvenci un dokumentāciju.

MĒS SAGAIDĀM

  • Pieredze datu analīzē / BI, vēlams 2+ gadi;
  • praktiskas iemaņas darbā ar Power BI (DAX, Power BI Service);
  • labas SQL zināšanas - spēja pašam rakstīt un optimizēt sarežģītus vaicājumus;
  • izpratne par datubāzēm, datu noliktavu principiem un ETL/datu plūsmu pamatiem;
  • vēlama pamata izpratne par datu arhitektūras procesiem, lai spētu koordinēt migrāciju uz BigQuery un runāt vienā valodā ar tehnisko komandu;
  • iepriekšēja pieredze ar Google BigQuery (tiks uzskatīta par priekšrocību);
  • pieredze datu migrācijas projektā vai datu platformas pārejas koordinēšanā (tiks uzskatīta par priekšrocību);
  • labas komunikācijas un sadarbības prasmes - spēja izskaidrot tehniskus jautājumus arī netehniskiem kolēģiem;
  • precizitāti, augstu atbildības sajūtu un spēju ievērot termiņus;
  • proaktīvu pieeju darbam un vēlmi iesaistīties procesu uzlabošanā;
  • spēju patstāvīgi organizēt savu darbu un prioritātes;
  • labas angļu valodas zināšanas profesionālai saziņai ar sadarbības partneriem un dokumentu izpratnei.

MĒS PIEDĀVĀJAM

  • Pilna laika darbu klātienē un attālināti, darba dienās plkst. 9:00-18.00, adrese: Laubes nams, Baložu iela 20A;
  • atbalstošus un profesionālus kolēģus, ar kuriem dalīties pieredzē;
  • aizraujošu pilna laika darbu dinamiskā, mūsdienīgā un augošā uzņēmumā, kur varēsi realizēt savas idejas un attīstīt potenciālu;
  • iespēju augt, attīstīties un pilnveidoties;
  • iespēju daļu laika strādāt attālināti;
  • sociālās garantijas, veselības un nelaimes gadījuma apdrošināšanas polisi pēc pārbaudes laika;
  • Bonusa karti 50 EUR vērtībā katru mēnesi (iespēja tērēt ēdināšanai, transportam, ārstniecībai) pēc pārbaudes laika;
  • papildus atvaļinājuma dienas (par darba stāžu, par bērniem, dzimšanas dienas brīvdiena utt.);
  • īpašas darbinieku atlaides MÁDARA produktiem;
  • iespēju ar kolēģiem satikties uzņēmuma kopīgi organizētajos pasākumos un atsevišķi komandu pasākumos;
  • Atalgojums (bruto): no 3000 līdz 3400 EUR.

AS MADARA Cosmetics ir vieta, kur ikviens var justies piederīgs. Kopā mēs veidojam daudzveidīgu, atvērtu un pieņemošu darba vidi. Sūti pieteikumu un esi, kas esi — tieši autentiskums un individualitāte padara mūs izcilus.

Mūs vieno vēlme darīt lietas labāk, vairāk, citādāk – MÁDARA ir vieta, kur tu varēsi augt, attīstīt talantus un atklāt sevī jaunas spējas. Augsim un veidosim nākotni kopā – vērienīgu, ilgtspējīgu un skaistu.

Doties uz visām vakancēm ⟶

Nepieciešams vairāk informācijas?

Mūsu HR partnere Sintija labprāt atbildēs uz taviem jautājumiem.

darbs@madaracosmetics.com

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Ocean Finance is a portfolio brand of Intelligent Lending Limited, a consumer credit broker powerhouse operating the TotallyMoney, Ocean Finance, CredAbility Luv Car Loans, and Binq brands. Our mission is to become the UK’s leading credit platform for people not served by the mainstream — helping them make informed decisions, access the right products, and improve their financial wellbeing over their lifetime. We do this by helping our customers make informed decisions and access personal finance products.

If our mission sounds interesting, please keep reading!




You’ll join the Central Intelligence Unit (CIU) within our Data team. CIU sources the most relevant intelligence and ensures its optimal deployment — through machine learning, analytics and decisioning that shape product and corporate strategy. Specifically, you’ll sit in the Ocean Squad, working alongside data scientists, analytics and AI engineers on the analytical frameworks, decisioning models that both elevate our product, and propel our marketing efforts for Ocean.




This is a hybrid role that sits between analytics engineering and data analysis. Roughly half your time will be spent building and maintaining the data foundations the team relies on — clean, reliable, well-modelled datasets — and the other half turning those datasets into dazzling dashboards and gleaning insights that directly inform business decisions.

It’s ideal for someone early in their career (a recent graduate, up to ~2 years’ experience) who enjoys both the engineering craft of making data effective as well as trustworthy, and the curiosity of digging into it to answer real commercial questions. You’ll learn modern data tooling and analytics practice in a fast-moving environment, with strong support from experienced engineers and scientists around you.

The mission statement of CIU is “To improve customer and business outcomes by ensuring the sourcing of the most relevant intelligence, and its optimal deployment”. We aim to be a leading force in creating value for the customer and the business, through best-in-class analytics and decisioning that shape the product offering, as well as corporate strategy.




A day in the life of this role could look like…

Analyzing complex datasets to uncover customer behavior patterns and product performance insights, translating these findings into clear and actionable commercial recommendations for stakeholders
Presenting insights and recommendations to senior stakeholders, telling compelling stories with data that drive action
Writing and optimizing SQL queries to extract and analyze data from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy and efficiency
Collaborating with business teams to understand their analytical needs and develop solutions that drive business value
Designing and helping to build automated dashboards and reports in tools like Power BI or Databricks to track key business metrics and support data-driven decision making
Build and maintain data models and transformation pipelines that turn raw data into clean, well-documented, analysis-ready datasets.
Creating and maintaining BI chatbots that leverage curated datasets to empower non-technical business stakeholders to self-serve
Analysing market trends and competitive landscape




You’ll also have the chance to get involved in other projects like:

Evaluating the potential impact of greenfield projects, such as the integration of new products, verticals, or customer journeys
Set up analytical framework for new products or enhance the existing ones by suggesting new KPIs / analyses
Understanding the key drivers behind past business performance and succinctly articulating the story to the board
Contributing to the design and implementation of our machine learning and predictive analytics initiatives




What we’re looking for

A degree in a quantitative or technical discipline (e.g. Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering) — or equivalent practical experience.
0–2 years’ experience in an analytics, data or engineering role, including internships, placements or strong personal/academic projects.
Solid SQL skills, and confidence working with data to answer questions.
Some exposure to a scripting language (ideally Python) for data manipulation and analysis.
An eye for data quality and structure — you care about getting things right, not just an answer.
Clear written and verbal communication; able to explain analysis simply.
Curiosity and commercial awareness — you want to understand the “why” behind the numbers.
Proactive and ownership-minded; comfortable in a fast-paced environment.




Nice to have

Experience with a cloud data warehouse / lakehouse (e.g. Databricks, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift).
Familiarity with a BI / visualisation tool (e.g. Looker, Power BI, Tableau).
Exposure to version control (Git) and collaborative analytics workflows.
An interest in the credit market, financial services or consumer finance.




Most importantly, you’ll live and breathe our values: Openness, Trust, Humility, Professionalism, Respect, and Passion.




Benefits

Pay you competitively and review your salary every year
Welcome you into our awesome team (dare we say the best? We do, yes!)
Bring the kind of vibe where we want you to share your ideas, even if it’s not “your area”
Provide you with all the kit you need to work from home. Of course, you can also work from our Manchester or Cyprus offices, too.
Help you plan for life after work with our pension scheme
Take care of your health with eye test vouchers, and our employee assistance programme
Give you 25 days holiday (to start with), as well as local public holidays and a day off on your birthday
Support you with training, mentoring and anything else you need to further your development and hit your career goals
Invite you to regular online and in-person team socials, as well as the occasional team retreat somewhere exotic
Help you help others with optional “community” days for volunteering
Provide you flexibility of benefit with the Ocean Health Plan

Title: Data Analyst (Dealing / Trading Analytics)
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

We are hiring a Data Analyst to partner closely with our Dealing team to improve trading and operational decision-making through data. This person will build and maintain analytics and reporting capabilities, translate business needs into well-scoped data solutions, and deliver actionable insights to support product evaluation and day-to-day operations. Strong SQL and Python skills are required, along with proven experience in trading/fintech/financial markets.

Responsibilities:


Partner day-to-day with the Dealing team to support trading and operational workflows with data and insights
Build and enhance analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation, trading performance, and operational monitoring
Gather business requirements and apply market/trading context to scope data requests, define metrics, and recommend practical solutions
Analyze large, disconnected datasets to synthesize insights and highlight risks/opportunities
Collaborate with business leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to enable market-facing solutions powered by data
Communicate findings clearly (written and verbal) and deliver solutions reliably and on time
Ensure data quality by validating inputs, monitoring key metrics, and improving definitions/documentation where needed



Requirements:


Strong SQL and database experience (querying, joining, modeling/structuring data for analysis)
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python
Basic statistical knowledge and ability to apply it appropriately in analysis and reporting
Demonstrated experience manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large, messy, and/or disconnected datasets
Strong understanding of trading/dealing concepts and market microstructure (execution, pricing, liquidity, slippage, exposure, hedging basics)
Familiarity with anti-fraud / trading abuse patterns and investigation workflows
Experience working with API connections (REST/WebSocket)
Experience with machine learning / deep learning / data science methods (nice to have)



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you - because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Supply Chain Master Data Analyst
Description:
The Company

Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.

We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.

Meet The Team

New ideas have a way of becoming not only great products but also best in class practices that can significantly enhance the customer experience. At Dexcom you can make a difference in the lives of millions across the world!

Reporting to the Sr Supply Chain Manager, this role contributes to the coordination of Engineering Change Orders (ECOs) and supports product lifecycle activities, ensuring that system data is accurate and updated in a timely manner. You will help enable effective planning, scheduling, warehouse and execution processes aligned with Dexcom’s operational standards.

Where You Come In


You will collect, validate, and update master data required for Purchasing (Buy Items), Production Planning (Make Items), Warehousing and Inventory processes
You will ensure data accuracy and completeness within ERP systems
You will support coordination and implementation of Engineering Change Orders across make and buy items, assist with updating system data in line with approved changes, Track ECO progress and escalate issues where required
You will assist in managing Phase-in / phase-out of parts, Product revision changes
You will ensure updates are reflected accurately in systems to support production and procurement readiness
You will provide accurate data inputs to support MRP and planning activities
You will support resolution of data-related issues impacting Supply Chain operations
You will identify and support improvements in data accuracy and processes
You will follow established data governance and change control procedures


What Makes You Successful


You have had exposure to master data, planning and purchasing processes
You have familiarity with item master data structures
You are an advanced user of Microsoft Excel, Copilot, Outlook, Teams and Smartsheets is preferable
You have experience in the medical device or CPG industry
You have experience with Oracle EBS, Oracle Fusion, PLM and Anaplan systems networks is advantageous


Experience And Education Requirements


Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field and a minimum of 2-5 years of related experience.


What You’ll Get


A front row seat to life changing CGM technology.
A full and comprehensive benefits program.
Growth opportunities on a global scale.
Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement.
An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve.


Travel Required


0-5%


To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Dexcom. Only authorized staffing and recruiting agencies may use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes on specific requisitions. Dexcom does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to the Talent Acquisition team, Dexcom employees or any other company location. Dexcom is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

#DexcomIreland

Title: Data and Insights Coordinator
Description:
Musgrave is one of Europe’s most successful family-owned businesses, with a rich 150-year legacy in food and brand innovation. We`re proud to serve communities across Ireland and Spain, feeding one in three people every day through 18 iconic brands like SuperValu, Centra, Mace, Frank and Honest, and Musgrave MarketPlace. By supporting thousands of retail and foodservice family businesses, we make a positive impact on the communities they serve. We`re committed to becoming the most trusted and sustainable business in Ireland, and we`re looking for dynamic, forward-thinking individuals to join us on this journey.

As we continue our journey toward becoming Ireland’s most trusted and sustainable business, we have an exciting opportunity for a successful candidate to join our team as a Data and Insights Coordinator.

Please note this is a 12 month contract.

What you’ll be doing:

Post Campaign Reporting


Management of Post Campaign Analysis (PCA) process end-to-end, ensuring



accurate and timely delivery of campaign performance reporting.


Deliver PCA reports within calendar timelines & agreed SLA timelines, maintaining a



high standard of data accuracy and insight quality.


Analyse campaign performance by combining sales data, shopper behaviour insights,



audience engagement metrics, and media channel performance.


Provide actionable recommendations and insights to internal stakeholders and clients



based on campaign results.


Present campaign findings and reporting outcomes to internal teams & clients as required.
Support investigation of sales performance analysis & ad hoc client-related queries.



Data Management


Manage shopper data for targeted marketing campaigns, including



targeted email and coupon activity.


Support execution of "Buy, Scan & Win" competitions in line with



campaign requirements.


Manage campaign barcodes, product & sales data required for reporting and



performance analysis.


Assist with store selection analysis, identifying locations with strong sales opportunities



for retail media activities.


Support data quality assurance and validation processes across campaign reporting



outputs.

Management of Case Studies Library


Co-ordination & management of Elevates’ case study library.
Transform campaign data into compelling commercial case studies for use in client



presentations (both existing & new clients).

Process Improvement & Automation


Continuously identify opportunities to improve reporting processes, workflow efficiency,



and data management practices.


Collaborate with technical & transformation project teams to enhance reporting capabilities.
Create process guideline & standard operating procedures to improve



efficiencies and consistency.

What we’re looking for:

Skills/Experience


Strong problem-solving skills and a team player, experienced in working under tight deadlines and in dynamic environments.
Experience in working with teams, building strong working relationships.
Experience with using analytical and data visualization tools (e.g., Excel, Tableau, Power BI, Looker etc.).
Operational experience with SQL or other programming language desirable.
Strong attention to detail and critical thinking to analyse, evaluate & interpret data.



Salary for this position is 37K per year

Weekly hours are 37.5

What we offer:

Career Development: With a commitment to your personal and professional growth, Musgrave offers numerous opportunities for advancement and learning.

Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a passionate team, where your contributions will make a significant impact

Innovation Focus: Be part of a company that values forward-thinking solutions

Community Focus: Be part of a company that truly values its communities and strives to make a positive impact.

Be Part of a Legacy: Join a company with a long-standing commitment to community and innovation.

Musgrave is an equal opportunities employer. We encourage applications from diverse candidates. If we can make any accommodations to enable you to be the best version of yourself during the interview, please let us know.

Musgrave operate a Work Smart hybrid working model where you can alternate your time between connecting and collaborating in the business and working remotely.

Title: VYRESNIOJI DUOMENŲ ANALITIKĖ (-AS)
HTML:
Ar nori tapti „Ignitis Group“ dalimi?

Šiuo metu ieškome naujos (-o) komandos narės (-io) duomenų analitikos komandoje. Šioje pozicijoje būsi atsakinga (-as) už analizę, elektros energijos prekybai vykdyti. Manai, mums pakeliui? Tuomet laukiame tavęs savo komandoje!

Take YOUR part in #EnergySmart!

Prisidėsi prie žalios ir saugios energetikos ekosistemos kūrimo:


  • Kuriant analitinius įrankius duomenų gavimui, analizei, procesų optimizavimui, procesų stebėsenai ir automatizacijai;
  • Analizuojant duomenis, ieškant dėsningumų ir teikiant įžvalgas verslo sprendimams;
  • Kuriant, testuojant ir tobulinant prognozavimo bei analizės modelius;
  • Kuriant įrankius kitiems įmonės specialistams, savarankiškoms ataskaitoms;
  • Vykdant didmeninės rinkos modeliavimą;
  • Inicijuojant ir diegiant naujus analitikos sprendimus;
  • Tobulinant ir administruojant automatizuotas prekybos sistemas;
  • Ieškant naujų analitikos, automatizavimo ir dirbtinio intelekto sprendimų.


Šiai pozicijai svarbu:


  • Aukštasis išsilavinimas (ekonometrijos, statistikos, matematikos ar kitų panašių tiksliųjų mokslo srityse);
  • Ne mažesnė nei 2 metų patirtis, kuriant analitinius sprendimus duomenų analizės ar modeliavimo srityje;
  • Geri programavimo įgūdžiai su „Python“, „VBA“ bei „SQL“ programavimo kalbomis;
  • Analitinis mąstymas, duomenų raštingumas, geri problemų sprendimo įgūdžiai;
  • Gebėjimas analizuoti didelius duomenų kiekius ir sudėtingas problemas paversti aiškiais sprendimais.


Privalumas būtų:


  • Ankstesnė patirtis elektros, kitų žaliavų, akcijų ar valiutų biržoje;
  • Su energetika susijusių duomenų dalinimosi platformų išmanymas;
  • Elektros rinkos veikimo principų išmanymas;
  • Patirtis kuriant prognozavimo modelius ar taikant dirbtinio intelekto sprendimus.


Mainais už tvaresnės ateities kūrimą, mes pažadame:


  • Papildomų naudų paketą pasirenkant: pensijų kaupimą, sveikatos draudimą ar kitą naudą iš pasirenkamų naudų sąrašo „MELP“ programėlėje;
  • Papildomas apmokamas dienas: sveikatinimuisi, trumpalaikės ligos atveju, savanorystei, už sukauptą darbo stažą;
  • Nelaimingų atsitikimų draudimą;
  • Hibridinį darbo modelį (3+2) ir lankstų darbo laiko grafiką;
  • Vidines karjeros galimybes;
  • Plačias mokymosi, kvalifikacijos kėlimo programas;
  • Dėmesį fizinei ir emocinei sveikatai: gerovės mentorių iniciatyvas ir psichologinę pagalbą, tinklinio ir krepšinio komandas ir t.t;
  • Galimybę prisidėti prie energetikos transformacijos, siekiant sukurti 100% žalią ir saugią energijos ekosistemą dabarties ir ateities kartoms.


Kviečiame prisijungti prie augančios tarptautinės komandos. Ją sudaro išmaniajai energetikai atsidavę specialistai, kuriuos vienija bendras tikslas – sukurti 100% saugią ir žalią energetikos ekosistemą dabarties ir ateities kartoms. O būdas tai pasiekti – tikrai ne laukti tinkamo laiko. Mes tikime, kad energija veikti įsikrauname patys. Suvienyti prasmės. Geriausi ten, kur svarbu. Prisiimame iššūkius. Drąsūs atrasti ir keistis. Tai mūsų vertybinės nuostatos, drauge jos kuria energiją, kuri yra visko, ką mes darome, varomoji jėga. Energiją, kuri virsta mūsų pasirinkimais. Jei ir tu vadovaujiesi šiomis vertybinėmis nuostatomis – lauksime savo komandoje!

Siekdami strateginių tikslų padidinti lyčių balansą „Ignitis grupėje“, pareigų pavadinimus darbo skelbimuose nurodome pirmiausia moteriškos giminės galūne.

„Ignitis grupė“ yra lygias galimybes užtikrinanti darbovietė, kurioje laukiami visi kandidatai, nediskriminuojant jų dėl rasės, religijos, lyties, seksualinės orientacijos ar lyties tapatybės, amžiaus, negalios ar dėl kitų su darbo vykdymu tiesiogiai nesusijusių požymių.

Pareigybių grupės pastovios atlyginimo dalies intervalas: yra nuo 3430 iki 5150 EUR (neatskaičius mokesčių). Konkretus darbo užmokestis siūlomas atsižvelgiant į darbo patirtį ir kompetenciją. Prie darbo užmokesčio gali būti mokama kintamoji atlygio dalis.

Apie mus:

Norėtum prisidėti prie projektų, keičiančių elektros energijos gamybos ir vartojimo ateitį Baltijos šalyse ir už jų ribų? „Ignitis grupė“ – atsinaujinančiai energijai prioritetą teikianti integruota energijos paslaugų bendrovė, apjungianti daugiau nei 4300 skirtingų sričių ekspertų kasdienėje veikloje besivadovaujančių vertybėmis ir vienijamų bendro tikslo sukurti 100% žalią ir saugią energetikos ekosistemą dabarties ir ateities kartoms.

Vystome mažai anglies dioksido į aplinką išskiriantį gamybos portfelį, didžiausią dėmesį sutelkdami į jūrinio vėjo elektrinių, sausumos vėjo hibridinių elektrinių, elektros kaupimo, konvertavimo (angl. Power-to-X) bei saugojimo technologijas. Pasitelkdami integruoto verslo modelio privalumus, įgaliname žaliosios ir lanksčiosios gamybos plėtrą ir planuojame pasiekti 4–5 GW instaliuotos galios iki 2030 m.

Title: Data Steward
Description:
Join our team as a Data Steward!

Yes, you can! That's our attitude towards our work, ambitions, ideas, and relationships. We at Luminor believe that anyone has the potential to achieve big things and encourage everyone to achieve them, professionally and personally.

Whoever you are and whatever your role is, with Luminor, you will get a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to build a local banking champion.

Data Stewardship team works on pan-Baltic level and is important part of Data Management initiatives at Luminor. We strive to improve, standardize and align data across all three Baltic states. Traveling in the Baltics is expected.

How You Will Make an Impact:



Develop and maintain Data Catalog activities, thus assuring that Data Glossary and Data Dictionary items are up to date, not duplicated, stitched to technical metadata and data ownership is assigned correctly etc.
Develop and maintain Data Quality activities, i.e., perform Root Cause Analysis (RCA), initiate data quality assessments, data quality improvement projects, create DQ rules, run data quality monitoring, collaborate with other teams across Luminor to fix identified data discrepancies etc.
Develop Data Lineage activities, i.e. provide required input to the Data Lineage, verify data flows, use the Data Lineage to make RCA, cooperate with Information Technology (IT) units to assure that all technical metadata are up to date etc.
Be responsible for Data Domain, Data as Product or Data Solution / Application
Serve as first point of contact in case of any question related to your area of expertise coming both form Business or IT
Participate in IT and business projects, Value Streams, ensuring data requirements are clearly defined, data quality standards are upheld, and data governance principles are embedded throughout the project lifecycle
Support business area and Business Analyst in analyzing incoming data requirements
Implement Data Management related processes, standards and procedures



What Makes You a Great Fit:



Educational background in Economics, Business Management, Finance, IT or any related field
Proven work experience as Data Steward, Business Analyst, Reporting Analyst, IT System Analyst or similar
At least 3 years’ professional experience in the area of data governance and data management
At least 1 year experience with banking or other finance industry, good knowledge of the Bank products, processes and IT systems
Very Good SQL knowledge, ability to work with large data sets to find quality issues, detect data structures etc.
Very good understanding of data warehousing and reporting concepts and ability to apply and use in practice data profiling techniques
Very good understanding of IT database structures/principles
Very good knowledge of MS Office and Project management tools (e.g., Atlassian JIRA, Confluence)
Ability to be a “bridge” between IT and business
Problem solver mindset and attention to detail-oriented personality in combination of possessing excellent communication skills, both written and oral
Initiative and high-level of responsibility
Experience in working with DWH, Tableau/PowerBI, Cloud Data Warehouse e.g. Snowflake, any ETL or Data Governance tool / solution will be considered as strong advantage
Data Management or Data Stewardship certification would be considered as an advantage e.g. DAMA
Fluent English is required in spoken and written communication due to working on Pan-Baltic level



Why You Will Love Working Here:



Gross salary. 2,750-4,000 EUR/month, which is to be determined depending on your level of experience and competencies
Flexibility. Flexible working hours, Hybrid work, and the possibility to work from anywhere in the EU, Iceland, Switzerland, and the UK (in total 90 days per year)
International teams. Teams that go outside Pan-Baltic borders, where people value challenging work together with good humor and having fun
Culture. You’ll be joining a team where things move fast, ideas flow freely, and change is part of the everyday. It’s a dynamic environment that keeps you learning, growing, and never bored
More vacation. Additional weeks of vacation are available to all employees who have been in the company for 1 year or more
Volunteer time off. We care about giving back to society, therefore, you will get additional days off for volunteering purposes
Paid leave. We are proud of our employees who are participating in military training. Therefore, Luminor offers 30 fully paid calendar days for military training every year
Health benefits. A competitive benefits package in addition to your salary that includes health insurance after the first 3 months pass in all three Baltic states, as well as Health days in case of your absence due to sickness without a doctor's note needed
Wellbeing. Access to tools and resources that help you feel good and be productive at work and in life
Professional growth. Internal and external training programs, LinkedIn learning, workshops, conferences, online training, etc
Special Offer for Luminor products & services. Enjoy special offers & pricing for products and services provided by Luminor



The application deadline is the 31st of July. We are looking for a candidate primarily already located within the Baltics. Please apply with your CV in English and PDF format!

Luminor is the leading independent bank in the Baltics and the third-largest provider of financial services in our region. We serve the financial needs of individuals, families, and companies. Just like our home markets of Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania we are young, dynamic, and forward looking.

Luminor has a strong and capable team that is committed to achieving our strategic objectives. We are dedicated to supporting sustainable growth in the Baltic region by providing a long-term commitment to businesses and individuals.

Title: Technical AIP Data Analyst
Description:
Founded in 2011, Altenar Technology Limited is a leading igaming software provider inspired by the vision of our customers. As our platform has scaled we have begun to generate high volumes of transactional data which can be potentially mined to generate additional insights. The Altenar Intelligence Platform (AIP) is the long term vision for unlocking this potential.




We are seeking a Technical AIP Data Analyst to join the Technology team to work closely with various Product and Business stakeholders to focus on using data pipelines and various analytical techniques to analyse business procedures. The role requires outstanding communication skills, proven ability to build and manage medium-scale modelling projects, define scope of existing infrastructure, identify data requirements, build methodology and tools that are well researched, undertake feasibility studies, and support with documentation. The candidate will be expected to demonstrate familiarity with concepts such as ETL, machine learning and statistics.




The successful applicant should illustrate comfort working in a startup environment, possessing high intellectual curiosity with ability to quickly learn new concepts/frameworks, algorithms and technology that powers Alternar business objectives. We seek someone with a high degree of ownership that can effectively examine flows of data, researching and evaluating to consistently deliver superior quality results on-time. The successful candidate should be able to rapidly prototype analytical models with the goal of generating strategic business insights as per the needs of the AIP roadmap.




Finally, the applicant is expected to display a high degree of independence working across multiple programs and initiatives to un-earth opportunities. The role demands strong data organisation, data structure, and data hierarchy knowledge, and may be suited to incumbents with extensive Data Analysis and/or computing programming experience.




The activities of a Technical AIP Data Analyst include but are not limited to:




Continuously updating core knowledge and skills in SQL and Python or R as applied to mathematical analysis
Liaises with internal stakeholders including the Solution Architect and Business Analysts, and directly with key Product and Business owners on topics for exploration and their description
Liaises with internal stakeholders including Data Engineers, Infrastructure Engineers and Data Services Engineers on data collection, movement and storage strategies
Analyse and Build analytical applications in a structured manner that can be tested and deployed based on stakeholder requirements
Undertake feasibility studies and various types of analytics including descriptive, diagnostic, predictive or prescriptive analytics.
Communicate the impact of technologies by delivering views, dashboards and report configurations using BI tools as required by internal stakeholders
Supporting software architecture processes with data handling and visualisation specifications
Participation in the Architecture Review process (including by providing advice and assistance) to ensure new initiatives or major changes to the Altenar product portfolio have data flow considerations represented at the inception phase
Examine existing business models and flows of data and design functional specifications by participating in the research, test, design of new data services tools for online or warehoused data
Work in a fast-paced, dynamic, multinational, multicultural environment
Creating and maintaining documentation for the solutions provided




Qualifications

Experience working with python and pandas
Strong SQL querying skills
Knowledge of noSQL and ETL operations considered a plus
Strong analytical and forecasting skills using offline methods such as spreadsheets
Strong math and statistics background (relevant Degree education required)
Proven experience with one or more BI tools (Metabase, Apache Superset, Redash, PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview)
Ability to quickly write ad-hoc scripts to analyze small to medium datasets.
Strong research and validation skills
Motivated and self-driven to work in an environment with minimal supervision
Strong presentation skills
Proficient in spoken and written English
Experience in Technical Data Analytics, Data Science (Computing), and/or Product Management roles
Graduate-level education in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other relevant field




Preferred Experience

Experience with Spark core functionality such as Spark SQL, Spark Structured Streaming is very desirable
Some experience in using Git version control in the context of documentation/source code management
Knowledge of Linux CLI tools to perform "quick and dirty" command line analysis to serve as first exploratory review of data
Some knowledge of MLaaS such as Amazon Machine Learning services, Azure Machine Learning, Google AI Platform, and IBM Watson Machine Learning
Experience in building, testing and validating machine learning algorithms
Prior direct or indirect involvement in data warehouse design, tool selection and support
Experience with Data Lab concepts with Hue, Zeppelin, Jupyterhub
Experience with Hadoop/HDFS - CLI, HDFS access from scripts
Knowledge of C# or Java is considered an asset
