Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Simpluris Inc. delivers comprehensive corporate, financial and legal administration services across the United States. Since 2007, Simpluris has administered over 9 thousand matters and distributed over $8 billion in funds. From class action settlements to corporate remediation, our nationwide team handles even the most complex matters with accuracy and transparency. Regardless of scale, Simpluris’ project management experts deliver legal administration services with speed, precision, and accountability.

About The Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Data Analyst to support Simpluris’ data operations. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing and cleaning client data before uploading it into our internal systems and performing calculations in accordance with business rules. You will apply your technical expertise to ensure data quality and accuracy, process information efficiently, and raise any issues to project managers for resolution with our clients.

Day To Day


Understand case requirements from the business and conduct analysis that supports business activities.
Present data-related issues to project managers for resolution with clients.
Participate in weekly meetings and daily stand-ups to coordinate on outstanding requests.
Read requests from the business with requirements provided by a project manager, conduct the analysis needed to support business activities, and upload processed data into line-of-business applications.
Interface with internal/external staff and clients, directly resolving issues with data (30%).
Analyze and clean client data (30%).
Deal with various forms and sources of data utilizing tools including Microsoft Excel(Lookups, Pivot Tables, Complex and Nested Logic, Date Math, String Manipulation), Python(Pandas), SQL (30%).
Assess current processes and make necessary improvements (10%).


What You Bring To The Table


Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or other related fields.
Experience with Excel, Python and SQL; familiar with handling data in a variety of formats including Excel files and SQL databases.
Ability to think critically about business processes and case requirements and understand how data can be effectively utilized.
Ability to communicate with technical and non-technical members of the organization
Demonstrated ability to solve problems and apply independent judgment and reasoning
Proactive and well-organized, able to multi-task and work under stressful conditions while maintaining high standards of accuracy
Strong mathematical and business acumen combined with a collaborative, team-oriented mindset


In keeping with our beliefs and goals, no employee or applicant will face discrimination or harassment based on race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, age, gender, marital domestic partner status, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, or veteran status. Above and beyond discrimination or harassment based on “protected categories,” Simpluris is committed to being an inclusive community where all feel welcome. Whether blatant or hidden, barriers to success have no place at Simpluris.

Important privacy information for United States based job applicants can be found here.

Title: Machine Learning Analyst
Description:
Bracebridge Capital, LLC is a leading alternative asset manager with approximately $12 billion of net assets under management. The firm pursues investment strategies primarily within the global fixed income markets with the objectives of capital preservation and absolute return without significant correlation to equity, interest rate and foreign exchange markets. Established in 1994, Bracebridge manages private investment funds that serve endowments, foundations, pension funds and other institutional and high-net-worth investors.

Approximately 170 employees operate from our office located in Boston’s historic Back Bay. The entrepreneurial and collaborative culture at Bracebridge rewards and supports motivated, dedicated, enthusiastic and intellectually curious individuals. We believe our firm’s greatest asset is the people who work here.

We are seeking a Machine Learning Analyst to join the growing data analytics and machine learning team. The team’s primary mission is to develop machine learning systems to answer open-ended investment questions and support portfolio management decisions. The team works across the project lifecycle and technical stack, from ideation, understanding and analyzing data, hypothesis generation and testing, and model development all the way through to the deployment and maintenance of models and systems in production. Our work spans statistical modeling, classical machine learning, and modern AI and LLM techniques.

The Machine Learning Analyst’s primary responsibility will be to contribute to these efforts alongside other team members. Over time, you will develop the technical and domain expertise needed to take increasing ownership of individual components and ultimately end-to-end projects.

The work is highly collaborative and spans quantitative research, software engineering, and machine learning. Analysts work with other members of the machine learning team and portfolio managers to translate loosely defined investment ideas into practical tools and models. Successful candidates will have solid programming foundations, be comfortable translating between qualitative and quantitative descriptions of problems and be excited to build data analysis and machine learning systems against the backdrop of portfolio management.

Since the team works closely with trading floor personnel to assist with portfolio management decision-making, an interest in economic and financial markets is essential, but no specific prior experience is necessary.

This role is open to candidates available to begin in the near term, as well as students expecting to complete their undergraduate degree between Fall 2026 and Summer 2027. Start dates will be determined based on candidate availability and, for students, degree completion.

Responsibilities


Collaborate closely with Machine Learning team members, portfolio managers, and researchers to translate open-ended investment questions into well-defined analytical and machine learning problems
Develop and evaluate data-driven machine learning and quantitative models, including simulation- and optimization-based approaches, for investment-related problems
Contribute to maintaining existing models and analytic tools in production
Over time, take ownership of individual features and components and full projects
Clearly document and communicate methods, assumptions, results, and limitations of models to other researchers and trading professionals across the firm
Stay current with relevant new techniques and technologies in machine learning and artificial intelligence, particularly as they pertain to finance and investing


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in a rigorous quantitative field
0-2 years of experience through industry internships, undergraduate research or thesis, or substantial independent technical projects involving software development, data analysis, or machine learning
Proficiency in Python and familiarity with the Python data science stack (NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, scikit-learn, etc), with experience in other languages a plus
Experience working with and analyzing data from multiple sources and in multiple formats
Familiarity with machine learning and statistical modeling fundamentals, including model evaluation and experimental design
Demonstrated ability to independently scope and execute open-ended technical projects
Interest in financial markets, intellectual curiosity, and comfort in working on open-ended problems
Strong written and verbal communication skills


Current anticipated annual base salary range: $110,000 - $145,000

Base salary within the range will be determined by various factors including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills and qualifications.

Title: Data Analyst Internship
Description:
About ConnectPrep: Our goal is to redefine "tutoring" - to use data, insight, and our extensive teaching experience find solutions for our students. ConnectPrep is about connecting - connecting students, parents, educators, and the community. We have bold ideas and an eye towards the future.

The Opportunity: We are offering a Data Analyst Internship for qualified candidates. This is a cross-disciplinary role, both instructional and technical. You'll engage with our team of educators to validate and iterate the models that drive our analytics. Our Data Analysts develop an understanding of our algorithms, the technologies and platforms that support our products, and our proprietary educational content. This knowledge will then be synthesized into actionable tests to build modules that will assist in identifying the academic needs of each individual student. You will engage with team members that think outside of the box, with the goal of improving education through the creation of a data infrastructure and insightful analytics.

Requirements

What we are looking for:


Core skills

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

Curely is looking for a Medical Receptionist to be the first point of contact for our patients. In this role, you’ll ensure a smooth check-in process, manage appointment scheduling, and provide administrative support to our medical team. If you have strong communication skills, a patient-friendly attitude, and a passion for healthcare, we’d love to hear from you!

What You'll Do


Greet and check in patients with professionalism and warmth.
Schedule, confirm, and manage patient appointments efficiently.
Handle phone calls, emails, and patient inquiries.
Maintain patient records and ensure all information is up to date.
Verify insurance details and process billing transactions.
Coordinate with doctors, nurses, and other staff for seamless patient care.
Uphold confidentiality and comply with HIPAA regulations.
Assist with other front desk and clerical duties as needed.


What We’re Looking For


High school diploma or equivalent (Associate’s degree preferred).
1-2 years of experience in a receptionist or administrative role (medical setting preferred).
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
Proficiency in scheduling software and Microsoft Office Suite.
Ability to multitask and work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.
Friendly, patient-focused attitude with excellent customer service skills.
Knowledge of medical terminology and insurance processes is a plus.


Bonus Skills & Experience


Bilingual in English and Spanish (or other languages) is a plus.
Experience using electronic health record (EHR) systems.
Previous work in a hospital or clinic setting.


Why Join Curely?


Competitive Salary – Fair and rewarding compensation.
Health & Dental Insurance – Comprehensive coverage for you and your family.
Paid Time Off & Holidays – Enjoy a work-life balance.
Career Growth Opportunities – Training and promotion possibilities.
Supportive Team Culture – Work in a collaborative and respectful environment.
Modern & Innovative Workplace – Utilize cutting-edge healthcare technology.

Location: United States only

Pay Rate: $28 per hour

No prior experience is required. Paid training will be provided. We are looking for dependable individuals with a positive attitude, strong work ethic, and a willingness to learn.

Title: Virtual Data Analyst Full Time (100% Remote)
Description:
Job Type: Full-Time/Part-Time

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Job Overview

We are looking for a detail-oriented and organized professional to join our remote team with a focus on customer support. This role involves managing data, handling scheduling tasks, and assisting with travel-related inquiries, making it ideal for someone who enjoys organization, accuracy, and supporting clients in a dynamic virtual environment.

If you thrive in a structured role that combines administrative work, planning, and customer service, this is the right position for you.

Responsibilities


Accurately input and update data in company systems and databases
Manage and organize scheduling, appointments, and travel arrangements for clients
Respond to customer inquiries via email, chat, or phone in a timely and professional manner
Assist with general administrative tasks, document management, and record keeping
Coordinate travel-related details, including itinerary updates, bookings, and confirmations
Perform quality checks on data to ensure accuracy and completeness
Communicate with internal teams and external clients to provide scheduling and travel-related support
Follow company guidelines and procedures to maintain efficiency in remote work


Qualifications


High school diploma or equivalent (Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree preferred but not required)
Previous experience in data entry, customer service, travel coordination, or scheduling is a plus
Strong attention to detail and ability to work with large volumes of data with accuracy
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Proficiency in using Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) and/or Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs, Calendar)
Ability to multitask, prioritize, and manage time efficiently in a remote setting
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently with minimal supervision
Comfortable using CRM software, scheduling tools, or travel booking platforms (training provided if necessary)
Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace to perform job duties effectively


Benefits


Flexible work schedule – Work from home with the ability to set your hours
Competitive pay – Hourly or salary-based, depending on experience
Growth opportunities – Potential for advancement into higher administrative, travel coordination, or managerial roles
Paid training and development – Learn new tools and improve your skills in scheduling and travel support
Exclusive travel perks – Access to discounted travel rates, hotel stays, and flight deals
Supportive virtual team environment – Work with a collaborative and helpful team
Work-life balance – No commute, the flexibility to work from anywhere, and the opportunity to assist clients with their travel and scheduling needs


If you’re looking for a stable, remote-friendly role that allows you to contribute to a growing company while also enjoying travel-related benefits, we encourage you to apply today!

Title: Jr People Data Analyst
Description:
Anduril Industries is a defense technology company with a mission to transform U.S. and allied military capabilities with advanced technology. By bringing the expertise, technology, and business model of the 21st century’s most innovative companies to the defense industry, Anduril is changing how military systems are designed, built and sold. Anduril’s family of systems is powered by Lattice OS, an AI-powered operating system that turns thousands of data streams into a realtime, 3D command and control center. As the world enters an era of strategic competition, Anduril is committed to bringing cutting-edge autonomy, AI, computer vision, sensor fusion, and networking technology to the military in months, not years.

About The Team

The People Analytics team is at the forefront of transforming how we understand and optimize our workforce. We are a dynamic group of analytical thinkers and storytellers who leverage advanced analytics and robust methodologies to create strategic insights from complex HR data. Collaborating closely with HR Business Partners, Program Management Teams, Business Operations, Talent Acquisition and various cross functional stakeholders we provide actionable insights that drives informed decisions across the entire employee lifecycle; from optimizing talent acquisition and development to enhancing engagement and retention. Our work directly shapes a thriving employee experience, fuels organizational growth, and ensures our people strategy is truly data-driven.

About The Job

The People Data Analyst owns the reporting and data quality foundation that enables our People Analytics team to deliver insights across Talent Acquisition and People Operations. You'll write the SQL, maintain the dashboards, run audits, and fulfill the ad hoc requests that keep our stakeholders informed and our data trustworthy.

This role is ideal for an early-career analyst who thrives on precision, wants deep exposure to HR data systems end-to-end, and is looking to build the technical muscle that underpins a career in people analytics. You'll work closely with our Senior Analysts — learning how upstream data work translates into downstream strategy while owning your own deliverables from day one.

What You'll Do


Provide critical support to the Senior People Data Analysts by executing standard data runs, owning reporting requests, and maintaining operational continuity across both TA and People Ops domains.
Write and optimize SQL queries against our data warehouse to extract, clean, and prepare datasets for analysis.
Maintain, troubleshoot, and perform minor enhancements on existing dashboards to ensure they remain accurate, functional, and up to date.
Own the execution of regular data quality audits across core systems (e.g., HRIS, ATS, LMS), identifying discrepancies and coordinating with internal systems admin teams to correct input errors.
Manage the intake and fulfillment of standard, internal ad-hoc data requests from various cross functional teams ensuring timely and accurate delivery.


Required Qualifications


1–3 years of professional experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or HR operations role.
Solid proficiency in SQL for data extraction, joining tables, and data manipulation.
Intermediate to advanced Excel skills (e.g., pivot tables, index/match, formulas, data cleaning).
Hands-on experience navigating and utilizing business intelligence tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) for data visualization and report generation.
Exceptional attention to detail with a proven track record of identifying data discrepancies and running quality assurance checks on large datasets.
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or technical field (e.g., Information Systems, Business Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Economics) or equivalent practical experience.


Preferred Qualifications


Familiarity with HR systems and data structures (e.g., Greenhouse ATS, Gem CRM, Rippling, Workday).
Basic understanding of core HR and recruiting metrics (e.g., headcount, turnover, time-to-hire, pipeline conversion, retention).
Familiarity with data warehouse concepts or version control (e.g., Git).
Familiarity with Python or R for basic data manipulation tasks.
Familiarity with Palantir Foundry


US Salary Range

$112,000—$149,000 USD

Benefits

The salary range for this role is an estimate based on a wide range of compensation factors, inclusive of base salary only. Actual salary offer may vary based on (but not limited to) work experience, education and/or training, critical skills, and/or business considerations. Highly competitive equity grants are included in the majority of full time offers; and are considered part of Anduril's total compensation package. Additionally, Anduril offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including:

At Anduril, we invest in our people. Our comprehensive, competitive benefits package (available at little to no cost to employees) ensures you’re supported in health, recovery, and whatever comes next. For more information, Explore Our Benefits.

Protecting Yourself from Recruitment Scams

Anduril is committed to maintaining the integrity of our Talent acquisition process and the security of our candidates. We've observed a rise in sophisticated phishing and fraudulent schemes where individuals impersonate Anduril representatives, luring job seekers with false interviews or job offers. These scammers often attempt to extract payment or sensitive personal information.

To Ensure Your Safety And Help You Navigate Your Job Search With Confidence, Please Keep The Following Critical Points In Mind


No Financial Requests: Anduril will never solicit payment or demand personal financial details (such as banking information, credit card numbers, or social security numbers) at any stage of our hiring process. Our legitimate recruitment is entirely free for candidates.
Please always verify communications:
Direct from Anduril: If you receive an email from one of our recruiters, it will only come from an @anduril.com address.
Via Agency Partner: If contacted by a recruiting agency for an Anduril role, their email will clearly identify their agency. If you suspect any suspicious activity, please verify the agency's authenticity by reaching out to contact@anduril.com.
Exercise Caution with Unsolicited Outreach: If you receive any communication that appears suspicious, contains grammatical errors, or makes unusual requests, do not engage. Always confirm the sender's email domain is @anduril.com before providing any personal information or clicking on links.
What to Do If You Suspect Fraud: Should you encounter any questionable or fraudulent outreach claiming to be from Anduril, please report it immediately to contact@anduril.com. Your proactive caution is invaluable in protecting your personal information and upholding the security and trustworthiness of our recruitment efforts.

Data Privacy

To view Anduril's candidate data privacy policy, please visit https://anduril.com/applicant-privacy-notice/.

By submitting your application, you consent to Anduril Industries using a third-party service provider to conduct pre-employment risk, integrity, and due diligence screening and assessing potential risks as part of your application process. This third-party service provider provides risk-intelligence services that may include analysis of sanctions and watchlists, adverse media, public-record information, and other lawful open-source or commercial data sources. This third-party service provider does not act as a consumer reporting agency. Use of this provider helps to ensure compliance with applicable laws and protect technology, intellectual property, and organizational security.

You'll work closely with our Senior Analysts — learning how upstream data work translates into downstream strategy while owning your own deliverables from day one.

What You'll Do

  • Provide critical support to the Senior People Data Analysts by executing standard data runs, owning reporting requests, and maintaining operational continuity across both TA and People Ops domains.
  • Write and optimize SQL queries against our data warehouse to extract, clean, and prepare datasets for analysis.
  • Maintain, troubleshoot, and perform minor enhancements on existing dashboards to ensure they remain accurate, functional, and up to date.
  • Own the execution of regular data quality audits across core systems (e.g., HRIS, ATS, LMS), identifying discrepancies and coordinating with internal systems admin teams to correct input errors.
  • Manage the intake and fulfillment of standard, internal ad-hoc data requests from various cross functional teams ensuring timely and accurate delivery.

Required Qualifications

  • 1–3 years of professional experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or HR operations role.
  • Solid proficiency in SQL for data extraction, joining tables, and data manipulation.
  • Intermediate to advanced Excel skills (e.g., pivot tables, index/match, formulas, data cleaning).
  • Hands-on experience navigating and utilizing business intelligence tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) for data visualization and report generation.
  • Exceptional attention to detail with a proven track record of identifying data discrepancies and running quality assurance checks on large datasets.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or technical field (e.g., Information Systems, Business Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Economics) or equivalent practical experience.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Familiarity with HR systems and data structures (e.g., Greenhouse ATS, Gem CRM, Rippling, Workday).
  • Basic understanding of core HR and recruiting metrics (e.g., headcount, turnover, time-to-hire, pipeline conversion, retention).
  • Familiarity with data warehouse concepts or version control (e.g., Git).
  • Familiarity with Python or R for basic data manipulation tasks.
  • Familiarity with Palantir Foundry

US Salary Range

$112,000—$149,000 USD

Benefits

The salary range for this role is an estimate based on a wide range of compensation factors, inclusive of base salary only. Actual salary offer may vary based on (but not limited to) work experience, education and/or training, critical skills, and/or business considerations. Highly competitive equity grants are included in the majority of full time offers; and are considered part of Anduril's total compensation package. Additionally, Anduril offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including:

At Anduril, we invest in our people. Our comprehensive, competitive benefits package (available at little to no cost to employees) ensures you’re supported in health, recovery, and whatever comes next. For more information, Explore Our Benefits.

Protecting Yourself from Recruitment Scams

Anduril is committed to maintaining the integrity of our Talent acquisition process and the security of our candidates. We've observed a rise in sophisticated phishing and fraudulent schemes where individuals impersonate Anduril representatives, luring job seekers with false interviews or job offers. These scammers often attempt to extract payment or sensitive personal information.

To Ensure Your Safety And Help You Navigate Your Job Search With Confidence, Please Keep The Following Critical Points In Mind

  • No Financial Requests: Anduril will never solicit payment or demand personal financial details (such as banking information, credit card numbers, or social security numbers) at any stage of our hiring process. Our legitimate recruitment is entirely free for candidates.
  • Please always verify communications:
    • Direct from Anduril: If you receive an email from one of our recruiters, it will only come from an @anduril.com address.
    • Via Agency Partner: If contacted by a recruiting agency for an Anduril role, their email will clearly identify their agency. If you suspect any suspicious activity, please verify the agency's authenticity by reaching out to contact@anduril.com.
  • Exercise Caution with Unsolicited Outreach: If you receive any communication that appears suspicious, contains grammatical errors, or makes unusual requests, do not engage. Always confirm the sender's email domain is @anduril.com before providing any personal information or clicking on links.
  • What to Do If You Suspect Fraud: Should you encounter any questionable or fraudulent outreach claiming to be from Anduril, please report it immediately to contact@anduril.com. Your proactive caution is invaluable in protecting your personal information and upholding the security and trustworthiness of our recruitment efforts.
Data Privacy

To view Anduril's candidate data privacy policy, please visit https://anduril.com/applicant-privacy-notice/.

By submitting your application, you consent to Anduril Industries using a third-party service provider to conduct pre-employment risk, integrity, and due diligence screening and assessing potential risks as part of your application process. This third-party service provider provides risk-intelligence services that may include analysis of sanctions and watchlists, adverse media, public-record information, and other lawful open-source or commercial data sources. This third-party service provider does not act as a consumer reporting agency. Use of this provider helps to ensure compliance with applicable laws and protect technology, intellectual property, and organizational security.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview:

The Master Data Analyst coordinates and performs day to day tasks for the creation, maintenance, and data quality throughout the lifecycle of a material for all Routes to Market, Business Units, and Bottlers. Develops knowledge and understands the end-to-end processes including impacts to up and down stream processes and systems. Supports the initiatives product launch process to ensure master data successfully supports flawless execution. Is responsible for data accuracy, performing and reporting on periodic audits and data integrity checks. Provides data verification and analysis in SAP material master and applicable systems. Reviews data input and processes to ensure quality, consistency, and timeliness. Maintains and supports relationships with all business partners related to material Master Data. Collaborates with functional groups on effectiveness and efficiencies of Master Data processes, including Sales Operations, Marketing, Supply Chain, IT, etc. Leverages data tools to analyze SAP and Legacy systems data to ensure compliance with business processes and a high level of data quality. Develops and maintains up-to-date and accurate documentation and provides knowledge transfer for business processes & workflows within functional area. Improves and streamlines business processes within functional area. Participates in and/or leads projects internally to include material testing and supporting business design requirements, if applicable.

Total Rewards:


Salary range: USD $62,500.00 - USD $80,000.00
Actual placement within the compensation range may vary depending on experience, skills, and other factors
Benefits, subject to election and eligibility: Medical, Dental, Vision, Disability, Paid Time Off (including paid parental leave, vacation, and sick time), 401k with company match, Tuition Reimbursement, and Mileage Reimbursement
Annual bonus based on performance and eligibility



Requirements:

Qualification:


Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution preferred
3+ year professional work experience in the field of Finance or General Business
SAP experience highly preferred
Proficient in Microsoft Outlook (using advanced functions in MS Excel such as pivot tables, V look ups and some base knowledge of MS Access).
Excellent attention to detail, accuracy, advanced analytical skills, quality, and strong organizational skills
An understanding of how to manipulate large datasets, combined with strong customer service and interpersonal skills
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, both quantitative and qualitative



Company Overview:

Keurig Dr Pepper (Nasdaq: KDP) is a leading beverage company with more than 150 owned, licensed and partner brands that meet a wide range of needs and occasions. Our North American refreshment beverage business holds leadership positions across carbonated soft drinks, water, juice and mixers with a portfolio of iconic brands such as Dr Pepper®, Canada Dry®, Mott’s®, A&W®, Peñafiel®, GHOST®, 7UP®, Snapple®, Clamato® and Core Hydration®. Our global coffee business spans more than 100 markets and includes the leading Keurig® single‑serve brewing system in the U.S. and Canada, along with powerhouse brands such as Peet’s, L’OR and Jacobs, and other regional coffee leaders. Our more than 50,000 employees aim to enhance the experience of every beverage and coffee occasion while making a positive impact for people, communities and the planet.

We strive to be an employer of choice, providing a culture and opportunities that empower our team to grow and develop. We offer robust benefits to support your health and wellness as well as your personal and financial well-being. We also provide employee programs designed to enhance your professional growth and development, while ensuring you feel valued, inspired and appreciated at work.

Whatever your area of expertise, at KDP you can be a part of a team that’s proud of its brands, partnerships, innovation and growth. Will you join us?

Please include the job title and location or Job ID # in the email subject line in order for your email application to be considered.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Senior Quantitative Analyst
Description:
Lead Quantitative Technology Manager

Hedge Fund Technology | Boston




VARO Partners has been retained by a leading global investment manager to identify senior quantitative technology leaders to help shape the next generation of front-office investment platforms across Securities and Risk Analytics.




These are highly visible positions sitting at the intersection of quantitative research, investment technology and engineering. Rather than building models yourself, you'll leverage your previous quantitative experience to lead the design, delivery and evolution of sophisticated investment systems used by portfolio managers, quantitative researchers and investment teams. Benefitting from significant ownership, close partnership with investment teams and the opportunity to take on leadership responsibilities immediately or over time.




The Opportunity

This is not a traditional Business Analyst position. We're looking for individuals who began their careers building quantitative models, pricing libraries, risk platforms or investment analytics, but who now enjoy solving larger technology and organizational challenges.




You'll act as the technical bridge between front-office investment professionals, quantitative researchers and software engineering teams, ensuring complex quantitative ideas are successfully translated into scalable production systems. Working across the full investment technology lifecycle, you'll influence architecture, platform design, prioritization and implementation while helping shape the future direction of investment analytics.




Responsibilities

Partner directly with portfolio managers, quantitative researchers and senior investment stakeholders to understand complex business requirements.
Translate quantitative models and investment workflows into scalable technology solutions.
Lead the functional design and implementation of front-office analytics platforms.
Drive delivery of large-scale initiatives spanning risk analytics, securities analytics, portfolio management and investment data.
Collaborate closely with engineering teams to define architecture, APIs, data models and system design.
Ensure solutions are scalable, performant, auditable and suitable for production use.
Lead requirements gathering, functional specifications, testing strategies and production rollout.
Provide subject matter expertise throughout the software development lifecycle.
Mentor junior team members and contribute to the growth of the function.
Help define the future direction of quantitative technology within the organization.




We're particularly interested in people who have experience building or overseeing:

Risk Analytics platforms
Securities Analytics platforms
Pricing libraries
P&L systems
Portfolio Analytics
Market Risk systems
Fixed Income analytics
Derivatives pricing
Quantitative data platforms
Investment infrastructure




What We're Looking For

The successful individual will combine deep quantitative knowledge with exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills. You'll be equally comfortable discussing stochastic models with quantitative researchers, reviewing technology architecture with engineering teams and working directly with investment professionals to define future platform capabilities.




Experience managing people is welcomed but not essential. Both positions (Securities and Risk) offer a clear pathway into technical leadership for candidates looking to move beyond individual contributor roles.




Why Join?

Opportunity to shape industry-leading investment technology.
Direct interaction with portfolio managers and quantitative researchers.
Highly collaborative environment with significant influence across front-office technology.
Clear progression into technical and organizational leadership.
Exposure to modern data platforms, AI-enabled tooling and next-generation quantitative infrastructure.




j.gates@varopartners.com

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics
Description:
RSAC is the premier series of global events and year‑round learning for the cybersecurity community. RSAC is where the security industry converges to discuss current and future concerns and gain access to experts, unbiased content, and ideas that help individuals and companies advance their cybersecurity posture and build stronger, smarter teams. 

Both in‑person and online, RSAC brings the cybersecurity industry together and empowers the collective “we” to stand against cyberthreats around the world. RSAC is the ultimate marketplace for the latest technologies and hands‑on educational opportunities, helping industry professionals discover how to make their organisations more secure while showcasing the most enterprising, influential, and thought‑provoking thinkers and leaders in cybersecurity today. 

RSAC is seeking a highly analytical, business-minded Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics to serve as the dedicated analytics partner supporting both Conference and Membership Marketing initiatives. 

This role will be responsible for transforming marketing data into actionable insights that drive acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth. The ideal candidate combines strong technical expertise with exceptional business acumen and stakeholder management skills. They are comfortable moving between strategic discussions with marketing leadership and hands-on analytical execution. 

The Senior Data Analyst will partner closely with Marketing, Conference, Membership, and Data & Analytics teams to build reporting frameworks, measure campaign effectiveness, develop attribution models, identify growth opportunities, and provide executive-level insights that influence business decisions. 

This role reports into the Data & Analytics organization while serving as the primary analytical partner to Marketing. 

What You’ll Do 

Marketing Performance & Campaign Analytics 

Serve as the primary analytics partner for Marketing, supporting both Conference and Membership business lines. 

Develop and maintain reporting frameworks for marketing campaign performance across channels. 

Analyze marketing effectiveness and provide actionable recommendations to improve acquisition, engagement, and retention outcomes. 

Measure campaign ROI and support data-driven budget allocation decisions. 

Establish standardized marketing KPI definitions and reporting methodologies. 

Attribution & Funnel Analytics 

Develop and maintain marketing attribution frameworks across Conference and Membership initiatives. 

Analyze acquisition channels, conversion funnels, and customer journeys to identify opportunities for optimization. 

Partner with Marketing stakeholders to improve lead generation, conversion, and audience growth strategies. 

Evaluate performance across paid, owned, and earned marketing channels. 

Conference Marketing Analytics 

Support conference registration reporting and performance measurement. 

Analyze registration funnel performance and identify conversion opportunities. 

Measure effectiveness of conference marketing campaigns and promotional activities. 

Support sponsorship analytics and attendee engagement reporting. 

Provide insights that help drive conference growth and improve attendee experience. 

Membership Marketing Analytics 

Analyze subscriber acquisition, activation, retention, and lifetime value metrics. 

Measure performance of email campaigns, content marketing initiatives, and member engagement efforts. 

Identify audience behaviors, engagement patterns, and opportunities to improve member retention and growth. 

Partner with Marketing to develop actionable audience segmentation strategies. 

Executive Reporting & Insights 

Design and maintain executive dashboards that provide visibility into marketing performance and business outcomes. 

Translate complex analytical findings into clear recommendations for senior leadership. 

Deliver recurring performance reviews and strategic insights to Marketing leadership and executive stakeholders. 

Serve as a trusted advisor and strategic thought partner to Marketing leadership. 

Data Analysis & Visualization 

Write complex SQL queries to analyze large datasets and answer business questions. 

Develop and maintain dashboards and self-service reporting solutions using Tableau. 

Partner with Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering teams to improve data quality, accessibility, and reporting capabilities. 

Support development of scalable reporting and measurement frameworks. 

Experimentation & Strategic Analytics 

Support marketing testing and experimentation initiatives through measurement design and analysis. 

Develop audience segmentation methodologies to improve targeting and personalization. 

Conduct ad hoc analyses to support strategic business decisions. 

Identify trends, opportunities, and risks through proactive analysis and stakeholder engagement. 

Application Requirements 

Core Requirements 

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Marketing Analytics, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field. 

5–7 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, marketing analytics, or related analytical roles. 

Advanced SQL skills with the ability to independently analyze large and complex datasets. 

Experience building dashboards and visualizations using Tableau or similar BI tools. 

Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake. 

Strong understanding of marketing analytics, campaign measurement, attribution, and performance reporting. 

Experience with audience segmentation, customer lifecycle analytics, retention analysis, cohort analysis and lifetime value modeling. 

Experience communicating analytical findings to business stakeholders and senior leaders. 

Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions. 

Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. 

Bonus Points  

Experience supporting marketing, growth, membership, subscription, media, SaaS, or event-based businesses. 

Familiarity with Google Analytics (GA4) and digital marketing measurement concepts. 

Familiarity with dbt and modern data stack technologies. 

Exposure to Python or other analytical programming languages. 

Experience supporting conference, event, or membership-based organizations. 

Experience designing and measuring A/B tests and marketing experiments. 

Success in This Role 

Within the first year, a successful candidate will: 

Establish themselves as the trusted analytics partner for Conference and Membership Marketing teams. 

Independently own marketing analytics initiatives with minimal supervision. 

Deliver executive-ready reporting and dashboards that drive business decisions. 

Implement scalable marketing performance and attribution reporting frameworks. 

Improve reporting speed, consistency, and analytical rigor across marketing functions. 

Provide strategic insights that influence acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth initiatives. 

Earn the confidence of stakeholders as a trusted advisor and thought partner. 

Location & Eligibility 

Please be aware that although this is a remote position, to be considered for the vacancy you must have residency in one of the following states: 

California, Colorado, Florida, Kansas, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington 

Benefits 

RSAC believes in investing in our people. We offer: 

Salary range: $110 – $125 

Employer‑subsidised medical, dental, and vision insurance 

401K retirement employer match 

Home office equipment stipend and monthly technology stipend 

Thirteen paid holidays per calendar year 

Flexible personal time off 

Annual employee bonus dependent on company and personal performance 

Annual company‑wide offsite 

Our Culture 

We believe that our differences make us stronger, and we are committed to fostering a culture of respect, empathy, and understanding. 

We are a fully remote team operating across the United States, giving our employees the flexibility to work from wherever they choose. 

Our team is passionate and results‑oriented, striving to achieve excellence in everything we do. 

We strongly believe in creating an inclusive environment that values diversity and encourages our team members to share their unique perspectives. 

We recognise that by collaborating and working together, we can achieve our goals faster and more effectively. 

Why RSAC? 

The RSAC team takes great pride in helping shape the future of cybersecurity and being part of an expansive global community. We’re always looking for imaginative and visionary individuals who share our passion for providing cutting‑edge programs that equip cybersecurity practitioners worldwide with the intel and knowledge they need to thrive and safeguard organisations against cyberthreats. 

Our Values 

Adaptability: In our ever‑changing world, we innovate through determination, creativity, and resourcefulness. 

Community: We bring people together and build trust by embracing the unique thoughts and perspectives of others with kindness and respect. 

Excellence: Because we are where the world talks security, we have the highest expectations of ourselves and our partners. 

Equal Opportunity Statement 

RSAC is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and diversity. We take affirmative action to ensure all qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

Marketing Performance & Campaign Analytics 

  • Serve as the primary analytics partner for Marketing, supporting both Conference and Membership business lines. 

  • Develop and maintain reporting frameworks for marketing campaign performance across channels. 

  • Analyze marketing effectiveness and provide actionable recommendations to improve acquisition, engagement, and retention outcomes. 

  • Measure campaign ROI and support data-driven budget allocation decisions. 

  • Establish standardized marketing KPI definitions and reporting methodologies. 

Attribution & Funnel Analytics 

  • Develop and maintain marketing attribution frameworks across Conference and Membership initiatives. 

  • Analyze acquisition channels, conversion funnels, and customer journeys to identify opportunities for optimization. 

  • Partner with Marketing stakeholders to improve lead generation, conversion, and audience growth strategies. 

  • Evaluate performance across paid, owned, and earned marketing channels. 

Conference Marketing Analytics 

  • Support conference registration reporting and performance measurement. 

  • Analyze registration funnel performance and identify conversion opportunities. 

  • Measure effectiveness of conference marketing campaigns and promotional activities. 

  • Support sponsorship analytics and attendee engagement reporting. 

  • Provide insights that help drive conference growth and improve attendee experience. 

Membership Marketing Analytics 

  • Analyze subscriber acquisition, activation, retention, and lifetime value metrics. 

  • Measure performance of email campaigns, content marketing initiatives, and member engagement efforts. 

  • Identify audience behaviors, engagement patterns, and opportunities to improve member retention and growth. 

  • Partner with Marketing to develop actionable audience segmentation strategies. 

Executive Reporting & Insights 

  • Design and maintain executive dashboards that provide visibility into marketing performance and business outcomes. 

  • Translate complex analytical findings into clear recommendations for senior leadership. 

  • Deliver recurring performance reviews and strategic insights to Marketing leadership and executive stakeholders. 

  • Serve as a trusted advisor and strategic thought partner to Marketing leadership. 

Data Analysis & Visualization 

  • Write complex SQL queries to analyze large datasets and answer business questions. 

  • Develop and maintain dashboards and self-service reporting solutions using Tableau. 

  • Partner with Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering teams to improve data quality, accessibility, and reporting capabilities. 

  • Support development of scalable reporting and measurement frameworks. 

Experimentation & Strategic Analytics 

  • Support marketing testing and experimentation initiatives through measurement design and analysis. 

  • Develop audience segmentation methodologies to improve targeting and personalization. 

  • Conduct ad hoc analyses to support strategic business decisions. 

  • Identify trends, opportunities, and risks through proactive analysis and stakeholder engagement. 

Application Requirements 

Core Requirements 

  • Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Marketing Analytics, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field. 

  • 5–7 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, marketing analytics, or related analytical roles. 

  • Advanced SQL skills with the ability to independently analyze large and complex datasets. 

  • Experience building dashboards and visualizations using Tableau or similar BI tools. 

  • Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake. 

  • Strong understanding of marketing analytics, campaign measurement, attribution, and performance reporting. 

  • Experience with audience segmentation, customer lifecycle analytics, retention analysis, cohort analysis and lifetime value modeling. 

  • Experience communicating analytical findings to business stakeholders and senior leaders. 

  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions. 

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. 

Bonus Points  

  • Experience supporting marketing, growth, membership, subscription, media, SaaS, or event-based businesses. 

  • Familiarity with Google Analytics (GA4) and digital marketing measurement concepts. 

  • Familiarity with dbt and modern data stack technologies. 

  • Exposure to Python or other analytical programming languages. 

  • Experience supporting conference, event, or membership-based organizations. 

  • Experience designing and measuring A/B tests and marketing experiments. 

Success in This Role 

Within the first year, a successful candidate will: 

  • Establish themselves as the trusted analytics partner for Conference and Membership Marketing teams. 

  • Independently own marketing analytics initiatives with minimal supervision. 

  • Deliver executive-ready reporting and dashboards that drive business decisions. 

  • Implement scalable marketing performance and attribution reporting frameworks. 

  • Improve reporting speed, consistency, and analytical rigor across marketing functions. 

  • Provide strategic insights that influence acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth initiatives. 

  • Earn the confidence of stakeholders as a trusted advisor and thought partner. 

Location & Eligibility 

Please be aware that although this is a remote position, to be considered for the vacancy you must have residency in one of the following states: 

California, Colorado, Florida, Kansas, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington 

Benefits 

RSAC believes in investing in our people. We offer: 

  • Salary range: $110 – $125 

  • Employer‑subsidised medical, dental, and vision insurance 

  • 401K retirement employer match 

  • Home office equipment stipend and monthly technology stipend 

  • Thirteen paid holidays per calendar year 

  • Flexible personal time off 

  • Annual employee bonus dependent on company and personal performance 

  • Annual company‑wide offsite 

Our Culture 

We believe that our differences make us stronger, and we are committed to fostering a culture of respect, empathy, and understanding. 

  • We are a fully remote team operating across the United States, giving our employees the flexibility to work from wherever they choose. 

  • Our team is passionate and results‑oriented, striving to achieve excellence in everything we do. 

  • We strongly believe in creating an inclusive environment that values diversity and encourages our team members to share their unique perspectives. 

  • We recognise that by collaborating and working together, we can achieve our goals faster and more effectively. 

Why RSAC? 

The RSAC team takes great pride in helping shape the future of cybersecurity and being part of an expansive global community. We’re always looking for imaginative and visionary individuals who share our passion for providing cutting‑edge programs that equip cybersecurity practitioners worldwide with the intel and knowledge they need to thrive and safeguard organisations against cyberthreats. 

Our Values 

  • Adaptability: In our ever‑changing world, we innovate through determination, creativity, and resourcefulness. 

  • Community: We bring people together and build trust by embracing the unique thoughts and perspectives of others with kindness and respect. 

  • Excellence: Because we are where the world talks security, we have the highest expectations of ourselves and our partners. 

Equal Opportunity Statement 

RSAC is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and diversity. We take affirmative action to ensure all qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

Title: SIOP Data Analyst - Direct Hire
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Department: Office of the COO

Reports To: SIOP Manager

Location: Full‑time Onsite — MetroWest Boston Area

Schedule: 5 days onsite (future potential for 4 onsite / 1 remote)

Target Salary Range: $80,000 – $120,000




About the Company (Disguised)

Our client is a leading provider of advanced manufacturing and automation solutions, operating multiple technology centers across North America. The organization specializes in custom machining systems, automation integration, and turnkey production solutions, supporting a wide range of industrial and high‑precision manufacturing customers.

Position Summary

The Senior Data Analyst is a key analytical partner within the Office of the COO, responsible for transforming complex operational, sales, and service data into strategic insights that drive business performance. This role leads the development of enterprise dashboards, reporting frameworks, and data models that support SIOP, sales operations, and aftermarket service functions.

The analyst collaborates closely with cross‑functional leaders to deliver data‑driven recommendations that enhance revenue growth, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the collection, integration, and analysis of data from CRM, ERP, and enterprise systems to support sales performance, aftermarket services, and SIOP planning cycles.
Design and maintain advanced dashboards and KPI reports across equipment sales, service revenue, parts consumption, and forecasting accuracy.
Serve as the primary analytics partner for SIOP, providing demand/supply analysis, forecast variance reporting, and scenario modeling for executive decision‑making.
Develop standardized aftermarket analytics, including service contract utilization, customer lifecycle metrics, and parts‑usage trends.
Perform complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational and revenue optimization.
Ensure data integrity and governance through rigorous validation, documentation, and quality‑assurance practices.
Collaborate with Sales, Operations, Finance, and Aftermarket teams to translate business needs into scalable, automated reporting solutions.
Lead ad‑hoc analytical projects and present insights and recommendations to senior leadership.

Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
Experience: 2–7 years in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics—preferably in manufacturing, distribution, or industrial sales environments.

Technical Skills

Advanced proficiency with Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Strong SQL skills for complex data extraction and transformation.
Experience with Python, R, or similar analytical programming languages.
Familiarity with ERP/CRM platforms such as Infor CSI, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP, or similar systems.
Understanding of SIOP/S&OP planning tools and processes.

Soft Skills

Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for technical and non‑technical audiences.
Strong critical‑thinking and problem‑solving abilities.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Proven cross‑functional collaboration and stakeholder‑management capabilities.

Supervisory Responsibilities

No direct reports; mentorship of junior analysts may be expected.

Travel Requirements

Minimal; occasional travel to regional facilities.

Physical Requirements

Extended periods of computer‑based work.
Ability to lift up to 20 lbs as needed.

Title: Intern, Business Intelligence & Operations - Fall Semester 2026
Description:
Boston Celtics Job Posting

Job Title: Business Intelligence & Operations Intern

Department: Business Intelligence & Operations

Supervisor: VP, Business Strategy and Operations

Job Classification: Non-Exempt

Job Type: Part-Time, 29 Hours per Week (September - December)

Summary

The mission of the Boston Celtics internship program is to educate its participants in all aspects of operating a professional sports organization. Through observation and practical experience, interns learn how to function in a professional environment that requires a high energy level, the ability to perform under pressure and the utilization of essential time management skills. Through these developments, the Boston Celtics strive to successfully prepare their interns to compete for positions in the professional sports industry.

Responsibilities


Assist in the development of SQL scripts used to build Tableau server data sources and validate data accuracy
Build and validate data visualizations in Tableau, summarizing data in a way that is straightforward and informative
Assist with data accuracy audits in CRM system, including day-to-day maintenance of data
Update and analyze leads in email marketing database
Assist in tagging and categorization of social media posts
Assist Ticket Operations in a variety of Archtics related ticketing projects such as pricing structure, data maintenance, financial monitoring, and third-party integrations
Process and report on ticket donations for Shamrock Dreams program to Community Engagement
Field inbound fan questions related to tickets and troubleshoot technical issues
Support various fan engagement programs and solving in-person ticketing issues at the TD Garden Box Office during home games
Research and present best practices relating to business analytics and operations in the sports industry
Other collaborative duties as assigned by the Director of Business Intelligence


Qualifications


Strong critical thinking, a growth mindset, an deductive problem-solving
Detail-oriented and thoughtful approaches to ensure accuracy and understanding of projects
Interest in developing ideas and projects from the ground up and being a self-starter
Enthusiasm to understand data, database management, and reporting and analytics
Ability to communicate and work with multiple departments and senior management


Learning Opportunities


How to build data visualizations in Tableau, including how to tell stories and how to use data to drive insights to key stakeholders
Writing effective and efficient SQL scripts used to pull, validate, and manipulate data
A macro-level understanding of the role data plays in an organization, including how data flows out of and into systems used for ticketing, CRM, and email marketing
The ins and outs of a CRM system that manages hundreds of thousands of customer records
Foundation training and regular use of Ticketmaster’s Archtics ticketing system, the most commonly used ticketing system in professional sports


Requirements


Must be a junior or senior undergraduate student or graduate student
Academic Credit
Ability to work scheduled home games and events


The expected range of annual salary/hourly rate and potential commission, if applicable, is included below. Where an employee or prospective employee is paid within this/these range(s) will depend on, among other factors, actual ranges for current/former employees in the subject position, market and industry considerations, budgetary considerations, tenure, performance, and standing with the company (applicable to current employees), as well as the employee’s/applicant’s background, pertinent experience, and qualifications.

Hourly Rate Range: $15.00

Banner Seventeen, LLC is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All employees and applicants for employment are afforded equal opportunity in every area of hiring and employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy (and pregnancy related conditions), gender identity or expression, citizenship status, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental disability, genetic disposition or carrier status, marital status, familial status, status as a victim of domestic violence, military or veteran status, or any other category protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

About Company

Throughout the Boston Celtics’ storied history, they have long stood for equality and respect, including drafting the first African American player, hiring the first African American coach, and playing the first all-black starting five. The Celtics have won a record 18 NBA Championships spanning six decades, including eight in a row from 1959-1966, and winning their most recent in 2024. In addition, 51 former Celtics players, management, coaches, or staff have been inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame. The team has been operating under local ownership since 2002.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Employment Type: Full-Time

FSLA: Salary/Exempt

Division: Business Development

Department: Business Development

Reports to: Director, Business Intelligence

Supervisory Duties: No

This position will support the Business Development, Business Intelligence and Consigli's Senior Leadership teams. The main function will to be to support in the searching, tracking, reviewing, and reporting of new project and business opportunities. The Business Development Analyst will research projects, companies, key individuals, committees, etc. from various sources, and track potential leads into the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) database, performing analytics and reporting when necessary. As needed, support our client satisfaction program (Raving Fans), and be involved with Company-wide Strategic Initiatives.

Responsibilities / Essential Functions


Be the Business Development/Business Intelligence main source for research supporting pursuits, Strategic Initiatives, Regional/Market needs, Various other research requests
Track and manage all Federal and Advanced Technology leads through online sources
Data Governance: Ensure data accuracy and consistency across our CRM system, with the growth potential of report creation and coordination with other systems
Participate and own portions of strategic initiatives that vary from office to office, cross-department, etc.
Data Analysis & Reporting: Collect, analyze, and interpret large, complex datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities.
Process and KPI tracking at it relates to department and regional needs.
The person will support registration of, and, miscellaneous industry networking events, as well as other duties as assigned.
Process Improvement: Drive continuous improvement in data quality, reporting efficiency, analytical tools, and dashboards across business units.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with IT, BI Analysts, and Data Analytics teams to enhance infrastructure and automate data flows.


Key Skills


Strong research and analytical skill set.
Outstanding interpersonal and public speaking skills.
Problem-solving: Ability to identify business challenges and develop data-driven solutions; interpret complex data and translate findings into actionable insights.
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; ability to present data-driven solutions.
Strong time management skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
Desire to learn and develop new skills.
Competitive, driven, and entrepreneurial mindset.
High attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.


Required Experience


Bachelor's degree in a business-related field required.
1-3 years' experience in a Business Development role or similar strongly preferred.
Familiarity with data management conventions and best practices.
Experience or knowledge of the construction industry is a plus, but not required.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office required.
Familiarity with a Customer Relationship Management (CRM) database preferred, specifically Unanet (Cosential).

Title: Analyst Reporting & Analysis
Description:
Category/Area of Expertise: Data-Science & Analytics

Job Requisition: 535634

Address: USA-MA-Quincy-1385 Hancock Street

Store Code: ADUSA Dist L&D-Executive (5156965)

ADUSA Distribution is the distribution company of Ahold Delhaize USA, providing distribution services to one of the largest grocery retail supply chains in the nation. ADUSA Distribution fosters a culture that inspires and empowers our associates to always deliver exceptional service and value. Our team is supporting the supply chain network evolution to an integrated self-distribution model of the future, serving leading omnichannel grocery brands - Food Lion, Giant Food, The GIANT Company, Hannaford and Stop & Shop. To learn more about our company and the other supply chain companies of Ahold Delhaize USA, visit www.adusasc.com.

What You'll Do (Role Overview)

As an Analyst III, Reporting and Analysis, you'll play a critical role in shaping how data drives decisions across our Distribution & Transportation network.

In this role, you'll go beyond building reports you'll unlock insights, influence strategy, and help leaders understand what's happening across the business and what to do next.

You'll work with large, complex data sets, translate them into meaningful stories, and provide clear recommendations that help drive smarter, faster decisions.

Why This Role Matters

Data is at the heart of how we operate and this role helps ensure we're using it to its fullest potential.

As a trusted partner to leadership, you will:


Turn data into actionable insights that drive performance
Support key initiatives across Distribution, Transportation, and Supply Chain
Help improve the tools, reporting, and analytics that guide our business forward


Your work will directly impact operational efficiency, strategic priorities, and overall business performance.

What You'll Be Responsible For

Turn Data into Insights


Analyze complex data sets to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities
Connect insights to broader Distribution & Transportation and Supply Chain strategies
Provide recommendations that help leaders make confident, data-driven decisions


Build & Enhance Reporting


Design, create, and maintain reporting that is accurate, scalable, and user-friendly
Deliver both recurring and ad hoc analysis based on evolving business needs
Ensure stakeholders understand and can effectively use reporting tools and outputs


Partner Across the Business


Collaborate closely with Distribution & Transportation leadership, Supply Chain teams, IT, and Data Governance
Gather and refine reporting requirements to deliver meaningful, impactful solutions
Serve as a key bridge between data and the business ensuring alignment and clarity


Solve Complex Problems


Perform advanced analysis to uncover deeper insights
Use business modeling to evaluate potential outcomes and guide decision-making
Proactively identify opportunities to improve processes, performance, and efficiency


Tell the Story Behind the Data


Transform large, complex data sets into clear, compelling narratives
Develop presentation-ready materials for leadership reviews and strategic discussions
Help leaders understand not just the data but the story, impact, and next steps


Drive Innovation & Continuous Improvement


Stay current on analytics tools and emerging best practices
Expand capabilities in reporting, visualization, and modeling
Support a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement


Be a Key Contributor


Provide subject matter expertise on analytics tools and reporting capabilities
Support key initiatives, projects, and cross-functional teams
Help champion change by using data to guide growth and transformation


What You Bring

We're looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and confident working with both data and people someone who enjoys solving problems and influencing outcomes.

Key Skills & Strengths


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with large data sets
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivots, etc.)
Experience with data visualization and analytics tools (Power BI, Alteryx, etc.)
Experience creating calculated expressions (DAX, Calculate fields, Excel Formulas, etc.)
Ability to query using SQL in Databricks
Knowledge of Power Automate and Power Apps
Ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
Strong storytelling and presentation skills
Knowledge of supply chain, inventory, forecasting, or distribution operations (preferred)
Strong organizational and time management abilities
Ability to collaborate, influence, and build relationships across teams
Creative, innovative thinker with a proactive mindset
Ability to navigate change and drive continuous improvement


Education & Experience


Bachelor's degree in business, economics, engineering, math, statistics, or a related field
Analytics certifications are a plus
3-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, or a data-driven environment


What Makes This Opportunity Exciting


You'll work directly with leadership and influence real business decisions
You'll turn data into insights that drive measurable impact across the network
You'll gain exposure to multiple areas of the business and expand your influence
You'll help shape how analytics supports the future of Distribution & Transportation

Address: USA-MA-Quincy-1385 Hancock Street

Store Code: ADUSA Dist L&D-Executive (5156965)

ADUSA Distribution is the distribution company of Ahold Delhaize USA, providing distribution services to one of the largest grocery retail supply chains in the nation. ADUSA Distribution fosters a culture that inspires and empowers our associates to always deliver exceptional service and value. Our team is supporting the supply chain network evolution to an integrated self-distribution model of the future, serving leading omnichannel grocery brands - Food Lion, Giant Food, The GIANT Company, Hannaford and Stop & Shop. To learn more about our company and the other supply chain companies of Ahold Delhaize USA, visit www.adusasc.com.

What You'll Do (Role Overview)

As an Analyst III, Reporting and Analysis, you'll play a critical role in shaping how data drives decisions across our Distribution & Transportation network.

In this role, you'll go beyond building reports you'll unlock insights, influence strategy, and help leaders understand what's happening across the business and what to do next.

You'll work with large, complex data sets, translate them into meaningful stories, and provide clear recommendations that help drive smarter, faster decisions.

Why This Role Matters

Data is at the heart of how we operate and this role helps ensure we're using it to its fullest potential.

As a trusted partner to leadership, you will:

  • Turn data into actionable insights that drive performance
  • Support key initiatives across Distribution, Transportation, and Supply Chain
  • Help improve the tools, reporting, and analytics that guide our business forward

Your work will directly impact operational efficiency, strategic priorities, and overall business performance.

What You'll Be Responsible For

Turn Data into Insights

  • Analyze complex data sets to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities
  • Connect insights to broader Distribution & Transportation and Supply Chain strategies
  • Provide recommendations that help leaders make confident, data-driven decisions

Build & Enhance Reporting

  • Design, create, and maintain reporting that is accurate, scalable, and user-friendly
  • Deliver both recurring and ad hoc analysis based on evolving business needs
  • Ensure stakeholders understand and can effectively use reporting tools and outputs

Partner Across the Business

  • Collaborate closely with Distribution & Transportation leadership, Supply Chain teams, IT, and Data Governance
  • Gather and refine reporting requirements to deliver meaningful, impactful solutions
  • Serve as a key bridge between data and the business ensuring alignment and clarity

Solve Complex Problems

  • Perform advanced analysis to uncover deeper insights
  • Use business modeling to evaluate potential outcomes and guide decision-making
  • Proactively identify opportunities to improve processes, performance, and efficiency

Tell the Story Behind the Data

  • Transform large, complex data sets into clear, compelling narratives
  • Develop presentation-ready materials for leadership reviews and strategic discussions
  • Help leaders understand not just the data but the story, impact, and next steps

Drive Innovation & Continuous Improvement

  • Stay current on analytics tools and emerging best practices
  • Expand capabilities in reporting, visualization, and modeling
  • Support a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement

Be a Key Contributor

  • Provide subject matter expertise on analytics tools and reporting capabilities
  • Support key initiatives, projects, and cross-functional teams
  • Help champion change by using data to guide growth and transformation

What You Bring

We're looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and confident working with both data and people someone who enjoys solving problems and influencing outcomes.

Key Skills & Strengths

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with large data sets
  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivots, etc.)
  • Experience with data visualization and analytics tools (Power BI, Alteryx, etc.)
  • Experience creating calculated expressions (DAX, Calculate fields, Excel Formulas, etc.)
  • Ability to query using SQL in Databricks
  • Knowledge of Power Automate and Power Apps
  • Ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
  • Strong storytelling and presentation skills
  • Knowledge of supply chain, inventory, forecasting, or distribution operations (preferred)
  • Strong organizational and time management abilities
  • Ability to collaborate, influence, and build relationships across teams
  • Creative, innovative thinker with a proactive mindset
  • Ability to navigate change and drive continuous improvement

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in business, economics, engineering, math, statistics, or a related field
  • Analytics certifications are a plus
  • 3-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, or a data-driven environment

What Makes This Opportunity Exciting

  • You'll work directly with leadership and influence real business decisions
  • You'll turn data into insights that drive measurable impact across the network
  • You'll gain exposure to multiple areas of the business and expand your influence
  • You'll help shape how analytics supports the future of Distribution & Transportation
  • You'll be part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement

Let's Build What's Next Together

If you're passionate about using data to tell stories, solve problems, and drive meaningful change, this is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.
